name: Full Progression (Step 0 → Endgame) — Milestone Route
description: Prerequisite route for Full Progression (Step 0 → Endgame), segmented into milestone episodes by the progression-router planner. Each phase closes on a quest/unlock capstone rather than an arbitrary skill number.

steps.phase: Toward Early Game (Tutorial Island through first Lumbridge quests)
steps.instruction: ★ Early Game (Tutorial Island through first Lumbridge quests)
steps.detail: Requirements met — start Early Game (Tutorial Island through first Lumbridge quests).
No food needed. Rotate style per level chunk if desired (see toggle hint). Keep feathers (stack), bury every bone on the spot.

hints.note: Set style to match the stat being trained; revisit each checkpoint.
Bury bones as they drop — free Prayer toward 22 Protect Item.

media.caption: Arrive at Lumbridge East chicken farm (train-attack-10)
Player at the coop; chickens are the kill target

steps.detail: Auto-opens on first login after account creation, live inside Tutorial Island's starting house (not a separate pre-game screen). Set body type, skin colour and clothing style, then confirm to enter the house. Every choice here is free to redo later via Makeover once off the island, so a speedrun-efficient line spends zero time deliberating.

media.caption: Tutorial Island start - Gielinor Guide's house (origin:tutorial:01)
Gielinor Guide at the starting house; the tutorial begins here

steps.detail: Inside the starting house. Answer his experience-level question ('brand new' / 'played in the past' / veteran). Answering 'brand new' or 'played in the past' unlocks the Adventure Paths task system, which changes where you spawn in Lumbridge later (see the Lumbridge-arrival step); veteran skips Adventure Paths. Listen through the welcome-to-Gielinor and talk-to-NPCs explanation and his mention of the settings menu. This is the only tutorial dialogue choice with a lasting mainland effect -- both answers complete this step identically, so it is not a fork in the checklist, only in where you land.

hints.note: 'brand new'/'played in the past' unlock the Adventure Paths mainland spawn; veteran does not. Both answers complete the step.

steps.detail: Settings > Display > Graphics. Set camera zoom distance to preference; leave 'Scroll wheel can change zoom distance' enabled for quick manual zoom; optionally set Screen Brightness / Hide roofs. Worth doing now (or during the Guide's settings mention) rather than mid-instruction, since zoom/roofs affect visibility of instructors and objects in Tutorial Island's tightly packed buildings.

Settings > Audio > General. Adjust Master / Sound effect / Music volume sliders to preference (also area music mode, looping, unlock messages, hit sounds). Purely a preference setting -- the wiki lists the sliders but recommends no particular value; no speedrun-specific audio guidance exists.

steps.instruction: Set controls and learn the dialogue-skip techniques
steps.detail: Settings > Controls: toggle Run via the minimap orb, or leave the default Control-key run/walk toggle -- largely moot on the island (short walks, no run-energy pressure) but worth setting once since it carries to the mainland. The two concrete wiki-grounded speedrun techniques for the dialogue-heavy instructors ahead: press Spacebar to auto-select 'Click here to continue', and press a number key to instantly pick the matching numbered dialogue option (mouse-clicking works identically but is slower). Grounding-gap disclosure: no wiki page titled a 'Tutorial Island speedrun guide' exists -- the wiki's only 'Speedrunning' article covers the seasonal Quest Speedrunning minigame, whose own Restrictions section states the tutorial is SKIPPED there entirely (players spawn directly in Lumbridge), so it grounds nothing about in-tutorial pathing/dialogue. These two controls, plus the Skippy NPC full-skip bypass (a lucky, non-reliable trivia -- see the Lumbridge-arrival step), are the only wiki-grounded speedrun-relevant material found for step 0; everything beyond is left '??' rather than invented.

hints.note: Wiki-grounded dialogue-skip techniques (Game_controls.s5) -- the only sourced speedrun material for Tutorial Island besides Skippy.

steps.detail: Exit the starting house through the door and left-click a tile on the ground to walk the path toward the Survival Expert -- click-to-move is the base movement verb from here on; arrow keys pan/rotate the camera (keep the map north-centred so as not to set off in the wrong direction).

Talk to Brynna, the Survival Expert, to receive a small fishing net, then use it on the nearby fishing spot to catch raw shrimps (Fishing xp). Instructions appear top-left; the skills interface shows current level/xp per skill.

steps.instruction: Chop a log and light a fire
steps.detail: Talk to Brynna again to receive a bronze axe and a tinderbox. Use the axe on the nearby tree to collect logs (Woodcutting xp), then use the tinderbox on the logs to light a fire (Firemaking xp). Nearby oak trees are visible but not choppable (need Woodcutting 15, above the level-3 island cap).

Use the raw shrimps on the lit fire to cook them (Cooking xp); eating them afterward restores health but is optional -- health cannot reach 0 on Tutorial Island. Almost every survival skill here caps at level 3 and everything gathered is lost on leaving the island, so a speedrun-efficient line does the single required gather/cook/eat cycle and moves on rather than farming extra shrimps.

steps.instruction: Bake bread with the Master Chef
steps.detail: Enter Building two (the kitchen) and talk to Lev, the Master Chef, to receive a pot of flour and a bucket of water. Use the pot of flour on the bucket of water (or vice versa) to make bread dough, then use the dough on the cooking range to bake bread (Cooking xp). '??' flag: the wiki's own wording on whether Lev replenishes ingredients only after the music tutorial is internally unclear -- not resolved here per own-words/'??' discipline; it doesn't block progression since dough->bread only needs one pass.

steps.instruction: Play a track on the Music Player
steps.detail: Open the Music Player in the kitchen building and select/play a track to complete the prompt. Reads as optional but is a required gate, not a detour -- completing it unlocks emotes and the run-toggle option going forward.

steps.instruction: Talk to the Quest Guide and enter the dungeon
steps.detail: Enter Building three (the quest house) and talk to the Quest Guide, who explains the quest-journal colour code (red = not started, yellow = in progress, green = finished) and the quest-start map icon shown on the minimap/world map. Go down the ladder into the Tutorial Island Dungeon -- shared location for both the Mining Instructor and Combat Instructor ahead.

media.caption: Tutorial Island quest house: the Quest Guide (ori-t-13-talk-to-quest-guide)
Player beside the Quest Guide; he explains the quest-journal colour code before the dungeon ladder

steps.instruction: Mine, smelt and smith a bronze dagger
steps.detail: Talk to Dezzick, the Mining Instructor, to receive a bronze pickaxe. Mine the nearby copper rock and tin rock (Mining xp each), then use the furnace to smelt the copper + tin ore into a bronze bar (Smithing xp). Receive a hammer from Dezzick and use it on the bronze bar at an anvil to smith a bronze dagger (Smithing xp). The wiki calls out pushing both Mining and Smithing to level 3 here as worthwhile -- almost nowhere else has ore, a furnace and an anvil this close together -- but extra reps cost time a speedrun-efficient line doesn't need.

steps.instruction: Kill a giant rat with melee
steps.detail: Talk to Vannaka, the Combat Instructor. Equip the bronze dagger (opens the worn-equipment interface), receive a bronze sword and wooden shield, then open the combat-options interface and pick an attack style (accurate=Attack, aggressive=Strength, defensive=Defence xp; or unarmed punch/kick/block for the same three). Attack and kill one giant rat in the rat pit. Health cannot reach 0 here (floors at 1) so eating is not required; the wiki also documents a 2-damage-then-retreat loop to farm extra melee xp by re-entering combat repeatedly, but that is a deliberate detour, not part of the fastest line. Hitpoints cannot be trained on Tutorial Island.

steps.instruction: Kill a giant rat with Ranged
steps.detail: Talk to Vannaka again to receive a shortbow and 50 bronze arrows. Equip the shortbow and kill one giant rat with Ranged (melee re-entry to the pit is blocked at this point). Talk to Vannaka a third time to close out the combat lesson (combat stats, melee/ranged recap, Wilderness overview).

Enter Building four (the Tutorial Island bank) and use a bank booth to open the bank interface and note the deposit/withdraw mechanics -- no items actually move here. The poll booth is on view but cannot actually be used to vote (it needs a higher total level than obtainable on the island, max 57); Tutorial Island's bank tellers themselves cannot be talked to or traded with, unlike normal bank tellers.

The Account Guide (Financial Advisor) in the bank building gives an Account Management menu overview: membership, worlds/world-switcher, store, bonds, inbox, name changer. Also mentions account safety (bank PIN, authenticator) -- no in-game action, redirects to Customer Support.

Enter Building five (the chapel) and talk to Brother Brace, the Prayer instructor, for the Prayer list, prayer points and recharge explanation. Bury any bones on hand for Prayer xp -- only possible if bones were picked up earlier in the rat pen, otherwise Prayer training is unavailable again until the mainland. The organ upstairs is playable but non-essential -- skip it on a speedrun-efficient line.

media.caption: Tutorial Island chapel: Brother Brace, the Prayer instructor (ori-t-19-talk-to-prayer-instructor)
Player beside Brother Brace; talk to him for the Prayer list and points explanation

steps.detail: Talk to Paul, the Ironman tutor, on the road to the final building, and choose an Ironman mode (Standard / Hardcore / Ultimate / Group) or remain a normal account. Mode is freely switchable while still on the island; after leaving, Adam in Lumbridge only allows downward switches (Ultimate/Hardcore -> Standard -> non-Ironman), and the choice locks permanently after one week. '??': the wiki does not detail the Group Ironman branch's internal steps beyond noting Group Ironman players continue to The Node south of the final building instead of the Magic building.

Enter Building six (the Magic building) and talk to Terrova, the Magic Instructor, to receive 5 air runes and 5 mind runes. Open the spellbook interface, select Wind Strike, and cast it on a nearby chicken (Magic xp). Only one guaranteed cast is allowed here before being told to move on; the wiki notes players can return to the rat pit to push Magic to level 3 (up to 32 of each rune, since xp is granted even on a miss) -- an optional detour, not part of the fastest line.

steps.instruction: Cast Lumbridge Home Teleport to leave the island
steps.detail: Talk to Terrova again. If planning Ironman Mode and it wasn't chosen yet, he asks before you leave -- backtrack to the Ironman tutor first if needed. Cast Lumbridge Home Teleport from the spellbook (auto-taught, no Magic level or rune cost, ~30 min real-time cooldown) to leave Tutorial Island.

hints.note: Only teleport a fresh account has; ~30 min cooldown, free of Magic level/rune cost.

steps.detail: Land in Lumbridge, north-east of Tutorial Island, with standard starting provisions. Spawn point branches on the Gielinor Guide choice (see the character-creation-adjacent dialogue step): outside The Sheared Ram pub near Adventurer Jon -- who immediately introduces Adventure Paths -- if Adventure Paths was unlocked and Ironman Mode was not chosen; inside/near the walled Lumbridge Castle courtyard otherwise. This closes step 0 (Tutorial Island) -- mainland progression is a separate domain. Closing aside, not part of the baseline route: an NPC named Skippy occasionally spawns in the starting house near the Gielinor Guide and can skip the entire tutorial outright, but he isn't permanently present (an anti-bot measure), so this is a lucky bypass rather than a reliable line. Trivia: island-wide caps are max total level 57, max total xp 3334.

steps.instruction: Land in Lumbridge and unlock world chat
steps.detail: Casting Lumbridge Home Teleport off the island permanently unlocks world chat and further skill training. Ironman-mode players are diverted to a separate Ironman tutor before the teleport.

media.caption: Arrive in Lumbridge after Tutorial Island (scenario capture)
Lumbridge arrival scene (scenario capture, oracle PASS)

steps.instruction: Buy a spade at the Lumbridge General Store
steps.detail:
First gear stop: Lumbridge General Store in the castle courtyard -- a non-GE named vendor that restocks on a per-item timer (sellmultiplier=1300/buymultiplier=400/delta=30). Buy a Spade (stock 5) here first -- it's mandatory for X Marks the Spot and not obtainable free anywhere else nearby. Shears (stock 2), Pot (stock 5), Bucket (stock 3) and Tinderbox (stock 2) are also sold here as backups, but Sheep Shearer/Cook's Assistant each hand out or spawn their own copies free during the quest, so only the Spade is a hard buy.

Travel budget for the first hour is walking only: Lumbridge Home Teleport (already used to arrive) resets you back to the castle courtyard, not useful for skipping travel elsewhere since it's the hub you start in. Every other Lumbridge transport option (amulet of glory, gnome glider, fairy ring, ring of returning, canoe) needs an item/quest/level not yet available. The Al Kharid gate costs 10 coins each way until Prince Ali Rescue is done (avoidable via a long detour) -- worth knowing as a coin sink to route around, though none of the four openers ahead require crossing it.

Opener order ahead (own-synthesis from each quest's own Details/startmap + Lumbridge's Quests list -- not from a dedicated speedrun source): Cook's Assistant and Sheep Shearer first, since both start/finish inside or immediately around Lumbridge Castle (kitchen, cellar, cow field, mill, sheep pen, 1st-floor spinning wheel all within ~10-30 tiles) with zero long-distance travel and a shared turn-in visit; The Restless Ghost third, since its only detour is the one-way Wizards' Tower basement trip and the rest (chapel/graveyard/swamp hut) stays Lumbridge-local; X Marks the Spot last, since its dig route is the only opener that pushes travel out to Draynor Village and Falador before finishing at Port Sarim, doubling as the first venture into the wider road network later quests need, with its 200gp + free-skill-lamp reward as a fitting capstone once the cheaper three are banked. Gap flag: no Xerxes/DunkingOreos speedrunner notes exist in this project's wiki-kb study set (those are YouTube creators, not wiki pages, so wikicli-only discipline can't source them) -- this ordering is grounded directly in each quest's own wiki page, not cross-checked against a dedicated speedrun source; flagging so a future agent with those notes can override it.

media.caption: Lumbridge General Store, castle courtyard (ori-m-02-buy-spade)
Shop keeper at his stall; buy a spade here first (mandatory for X Marks the Spot)

steps.detail: One-time low-tier gather before the prayer-pot-supply supply loop is established. Use drops (Hill Giants, slayer tasks) or NPC spawns. No rates known — "??" until measured.

mapMarkers.label: Grimy guam leaf spawn, Deep Wilderness Dungeon (mapID 26)

steps.instruction: Buy pineapples from Catherby charter ship
steps.detail: Purchase pineapples from the charter ship NPC at Catherby docks. Non-GE source. ~100 per visit depending on stock.

mapMarkers.label: Trader Crewmember (Catherby docks) -- corrects existing marker (2834,3445) 'Catherby', which lands off the actual NPC pin

hints.note: Trader Crewmember Stock table (Trader_Crewmember.s3): Pineapple stock=15/restock=100s. A 30-pineapple ultracompost batch (per setup-ultracompost's recipe) needs either two visits ~50min apart or a world-hop to refill a second Trader Crewmember's independent stock (16 dock locations total, per Trader_Crewmember.s1).

steps.detail: Fill compost bins with 30 pineapples then add 15 volcanic ash per bin to produce ultracompost. Ahead-of-time stockpile before herb runs.

mapMarkers.label: Ectofuntus (Port Phasmatys) -- for the step's own location.zone label
Nearest real compost bin, west of Port Phasmatys (Compost_bin.s1 map pin)

hints.note: Direct method (no bin needed): use 2 volcanic ash on 1 bucket of already-made supercompost -> 1 bucket ultracompost, 2 ticks, 1.1 farming xp. Fastest per-bucket path if supercompost buckets are already on hand.

Compost-bin method: bin already containing supercompost + 25 volcanic ash (consumed regardless of bin fill level) -> collect 15 buckets of ultracompost via 15 empty buckets, 10 xp/bucket. Big Compost Bin (Farming Guild, 45 Farming) doubles every number: 30 buckets, 50 ash, 30 buckets to collect.

steps.detail: Withdraw: coins (charter fare + 30 pineapples, total ??), Digsite pendant or Fossil Island transport (??). No other inventory needed — pineapples and ash fill en route.

mapMarkers.label: Fossil Island Volcano (ash-pile mining site, per gather-volcanic-ash)

hints.note: Keep a charged pendant banked separately for repeat runs.

Fossil_Island s1: digsite pendant is the fastest (teleports near the Digsite barge, speak to the barge guard to fast-travel to Museum Camp; can also be charged on the House on the Hill strange machine for a repeatable house-teleport option). Without a pendant: Digsite teleport scroll (lands SW of the barge) or a gnome glider to Lemanto Andra (SW of the barge, reachable via ring of dueling->Emir's Arena glider, Al Kharid gem-trader glider, or a royal seed pod to the Grand Tree glider after Monkey Madness II).

media.caption: Draynor bank: player adjacent to the bank booths (withdraw loadout here)
Bank booths + bankers; the generic bank-withdraw scene

steps.instruction: Fill a compost bin at the Falador allotment
steps.detail: Put 15 weeds (or other suitable produce) plus 15 empty buckets into the compost bin beside the Falador cabbage patch and wait ~60 minutes for it to rot into 15 buckets of compost -- the wiki's own ironman-focused note names this exact patch as a free, no-purchase compost source, matching the gather/produce-not-buy convention used across this pipeline.

mapMarkers.label: Falador herb patch (compost bin sits at the adjacent cabbage allotment, exact bin tile not pinned on the page)

steps.detail: Plant potato, onion, or cabbage allotments; collect produce. Check at natural banking breaks — do not interrupt active training.

mapMarkers.label: Falador allotment patch (south of Falador, gardener Elstan)
Catherby allotment patch (north of Catherby, gardener Dantaera)
Ardougne allotment patch (north of Ardougne, gardener Kragen)
Hosidius allotment patch (SW corner of Hosidius, gardener Marisi)
Port Phasmatys allotment patch (west of Port Phasmatys, gardener Lyra)

steps.instruction: Gather snape grass at Waterbirth Island
steps.detail: Pick snape grass spawns on Waterbirth Island. No quest gate. JIT batch just before a brewing session.

mapMarkers.label: Snape grass spawn cluster (Waterbirth Island, one of 26 mapped tiles)

hints.note: No respawn-timer or spawns-per-hour figure stated on the item page; treat as a fixed-tile ground-spawn walk loop, not RNG drops.

steps.detail: Buy vial-of-water packs from a herblore shop vendor (vendor ?? — verify non-GE source) OR blow vials from molten glass (Crafting 33) and fill at a fountain. One vial per potion.

mapMarkers.label: Jatix's Herblore Shop (Taverley) -- centroid of the shop's {{Map|mtype=polygon}} footprint

hints.note: Jatix's Herblore Shop Stock table (StoreTableHead sellmultiplier=1000 buymultiplier=700 delta=30): Vial of water stock=750/restock=100s, or buy the Water-filled vial pack (stock=750/restock=50s) for a batched purchase. A single 14-vial brewing run (per pps-05-withdraw-brew's loadout) is far under base stock -- no world-hop needed.

media.caption: Jatix's Herblore Shop, Taverley (pps-04-source-vials)
Jatix at his shop; stock vials of water non-GE

steps.detail: Withdraw: 14 grimy ranarr + 14 vials of water (clean-then-unf in one sitting; two half-inventories per load). Snape grass stays banked until the unf potions are made.

hints.note: Clean while walking to the bank — zero-time embed.
Herb cleaning and unf-mixing are click-paced; pair with bank-adjacent idle.

media.caption: Lumbridge Castle bank, third floor (pps-05-withdraw-brew)
Player at the Lumbridge bank booth; withdraw the brewing loadout

steps.detail: Deposit all prayer potions. Chain is 'done' for now when banked stock meets the active goal's demand (e.g., barrows: 20× prayer_potion_4 per SYNTHESIS goals example); otherwise the RECURRING herb-run loop keeps firing.

Withdraw: seed dibber, spade, rake, magic secateurs if owned (optional), N ranarr seeds + N ultracompost (N = patch count), run teleports (Explorer's ring → Falador patch ??, Camelot → Catherby, Ardougne cloak/tele, Xeric's talisman → Hosidius ??).

mapMarkers.label: Catherby herb patch (Camelot landing, run east)
Farming Guild herb patch (optional 5th leg, Farming 65 required)

hints.note: Route order = list order; skip any patch whose teleport is locked.

steps.detail: Unlocks Morytania region access. Required before Barrows and Slayer tasks there.

hints.note: start: ask about the temple to Saradomin
Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

media.caption: Varrock Palace: King Roald (quest-priest-in-peril start)
Player beside King Roald; ask about the temple to Saradomin to start the quest

atoms.label: Talk to King Roald (start Priest in Peril)
atoms.detail: King Roald is in Varrock Palace; ask about a quest and he sends you to check on the missing priest Drezel.

atoms.label: Go to the temple east of Varrock, by the river
atoms.detail: Bring a ranged or melee weapon -- magic attacks and the Dwarf multicannon will not damage the Temple Guardian ahead.

atoms.label: Fight past the Temple Guardian
atoms.detail: Click the large door, climb down the trapdoor to the north, then kill the Temple Guardian blocking the way.

atoms.label: Report back to King Roald
atoms.detail: Go back to Varrock Palace and speak to King Roald again before returning to the temple.

atoms.label: Gather 25 unnoted essence
atoms.detail: Collect 25 unnoted pure or rune essence, plus a bucket, a weapon, and 1 free inventory slot. The Quick guide doesn't cite a specific essence-mine route here -- flagged "??". Low-level players may need two trips; the Monk of Zamorak ahead can be safespotted over the pews with ranged or magic.

atoms.label: Climb to Drezel's cell and talk to him
atoms.detail: Enter the temple again, go up the stairs then the ladder to the 2nd floor, and talk to Drezel through the cell bars.

atoms.label: Kill the Monk of Zamorak for a golden key
atoms.detail: Kill the level 30 Monk of Zamorak on the 1st floor of the temple to obtain a golden key.

atoms.label: Study the monuments for the one that takes the golden key
atoms.detail: Go back to the trapdoor where the Temple Guardian was fought, climb down, go through the first door, and check the monuments until finding the one the golden key fits.

atoms.label: Use the golden key on the monument
atoms.detail: Use the golden key on the correct monument to open it. The Quick guide doesn't explicitly narrate where the Iron key (used later on Drezel's cell door) comes from -- modeled as the contents of this monument since it's the only sourced key between here and the cell door; flagged for confirmation.

atoms.label: Fill the bucket at the well among the monuments
atoms.detail: Use the bucket on the well in the middle of the monument room. The Quick guide calls the result a 'blessed bucket of water' with no separate blessing step shown -- flagged "??" on the exact trigger, modeled as produced directly by filling the bucket here.

atoms.label: Unlock Drezel's cell and pour the water on the coffin
atoms.detail: Go back up to the top floor where Drezel is held. Use the Iron key on the cell door, talk to Drezel, then use the blessed bucket of water on the coffin in his cell.

atoms.label: Talk to Drezel again to start The River Salve
atoms.detail: Speak to Drezel once more at his cell to move into the final segment.

atoms.label: Go through the monument room's eastern door and talk to Drezel
atoms.detail: Go back down the trapdoor, through the monument room, and through the eastern door; Drezel is waiting there.

atoms.label: Gather 50 unnoted essence for Drezel
atoms.detail: Collect 50 unnoted pure or rune essence to hand over; without essence pouches this takes a few trips.

atoms.label: Give Drezel the 50 essence to finish the quest
atoms.detail: Hand the 50 unnoted essence to Drezel to complete Priest in Peril and unlock Morytania.

checkpoints.label: Priest in Peril: start -> Temple Guardian
The vampyre (essence, golden key, iron key, coffin)

steps.detail: Unlocks the Mort Myre Swamp and nature grotto. Required for Fairytale I - Growing Pains.

hints.note: start: ask about Filliman Tarlock
Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Obtain a silver bar
atoms.detail: Buy a silver bar from the Silver merchant in Ardougne (an NPC shop, not the Grand Exchange) — the quest's own item note names this as the sanctioned non-GE source.

atoms.label: Obtain a sickle mould
atoms.detail: Buy a sickle mould from an NPC crafting-supplies shop (e.g. Rommik's Crafty Supplies in Rimmington).

atoms.label: Smith the silver sickle
atoms.detail: Use the silver bar with the sickle mould at any furnace (Crafting 18, boostable) to smith a silver sickle for 50 Crafting XP.

atoms.label: Ask Drezel about Filliman Tarlock
atoms.detail: Talk to Drezel in the Paterdomus basement and accept his request to find the druid Filliman Tarlock in Mort Myre Swamp; he hands over three meat pies and three apple pies for the ghost.

atoms.label: Travel to the Nature Grotto
atoms.detail: Leave the temple and run south through Mort Myre Swamp to the Nature Grotto north-west of Mort'ton. You must enter the swamp through the gate or Filliman never appears.

atoms.label: Meet the ghost Filliman Tarlock
atoms.detail: Attempt to enter the grotto to make Filliman appear; wear the ghostspeak amulet and ask how long he's been a ghost. He vanishes if you stop interacting with him, so keep talking.

atoms.label: Find the mirror under the washing bowl
atoms.detail: Take the washing bowl off the nearby bench to find a mirror underneath it.

atoms.label: Show Filliman the mirror
atoms.detail: Use the mirror on Filliman; he realises he's a ghost and asks you to find his journal (if using it does nothing, talk to him again first).

atoms.label: Retrieve Filliman's journal
atoms.detail: Search (right-click) the grotto tree to find Filliman's journal, then use it on him to return it; he teaches you a druidic spell.

atoms.label: Ask Drezel for a blessing
atoms.detail: Run back to Drezel and ask him to bless you. (UIM note: if Wolfbane was discarded, take the replacement dagger first, then speak to him.)

atoms.label: Cast the druidic spell on a rotting log
atoms.detail: Enter the swamp, stand next to a rotting log, and cast the druidic spell Filliman taught you.

atoms.label: Pick the mushroom from the log
atoms.detail: Quickly pick the mushroom that grows from the log after the spell is cast, then bring it back to Filliman's camp.

atoms.label: Solve the three-stone puzzle
atoms.detail: Make sure Filliman is standing outside between all three stones. Use the mushroom on the light-brown stone, the used spell card on the grey stone, then stand on the orange stone and tell him you've solved the puzzle.

atoms.label: Give the nature spirit your sickle
atoms.detail: Enter the hole under the tree, search the grotto in the middle of the cave, then give the nature spirit your silver sickle by talking to him; he blesses it and hands you a druid pouch.

atoms.label: Fill the druid pouch with swamp plants
atoms.detail: Leave the grotto and stand next to rotting logs, bushes, or branches in the swamp. Use the silver sickle (b)'s Bloom ability (costs prayer points) to harvest fungi/saplings/pears and fill the druid pouch.

atoms.label: Kill three Ghasts
atoms.detail: Use your filled druid pouch on a Ghast to make it attackable, then kill three of them (level 30 each).

atoms.label: Complete the grotto restoration
atoms.detail: Return to the nature spirit via the grotto in the cave; he redecorates the grotto and the quest completes. If aiming for the Morytania Diary, pray at the altar here first.

checkpoints.label: The Nature Spirit: bless the sickle
Killing the Ghasts: finish the quest

steps.instruction: Complete Tale of the Righteous (Zeah intro)
steps.detail: One of several quests enabling full Zeah access and CoX entry.

hints.note: start: ask about the expedition
Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Go to Phileas Rimor's house
atoms.detail: Head to the house west of the Cloak and Stagger tavern in Shayzien (from Xeric's Talisman - Xeric's Lookout, run north-west; fairy ring code DJR also reaches Shayzien).

atoms.label: Agree to help Phileas Rimor
atoms.detail: Speak to Phileas Rimor and agree to help with his investigation.

atoms.label: Ready a ranged, melee, and magic loadout
atoms.detail: Make sure you're carrying a ranged weapon, a melee weapon (a pickaxe counts), and runes for a magic attack — needed for the Tower of Magic puzzle ahead. Own combat gear, not a quest-specific item to source.

atoms.label: Travel to the Arceuus Library
atoms.detail: Travel to the Arceuus Library via fairy ring code CIS or the Lovakengj Minecart Network.

atoms.label: Ask Archeio for the Historical Archive
atoms.detail: Speak to Archeio, who teleports you to the Library Historical Archive.

atoms.label: Ask Pagida about King Shayzien VII
atoms.detail: Speak to Pagida, ask about King Shayzien VII, and agree to be teleported into the Tower of Magic prison.

atoms.label: Push the device and attack with magic
atoms.detail: Push the strange device all the way west, then attack it with magic from the north side.

atoms.label: Attack the device with melee
atoms.detail: From the south side, attack the device using melee. Make sure NPC attack options aren't set to hidden or the option won't appear.

atoms.label: Push the device east and attack with ranged
atoms.detail: Push the device all the way east, then from the south side attack it using ranged.

atoms.label: Investigate the skeleton
atoms.detail: Investigate the skeleton in the northern cell.

atoms.label: Report back to Phileas Rimor
atoms.detail: Return to the quest start and talk to Phileas Rimor again (Xeric's Talisman - Xeric's Lookout, run north-west).

atoms.label: Head to the War Tent
atoms.detail: Go north-west to the War Tent in the south-west corner of the Shayzien Encampment and climb upstairs.

atoms.label: Speak to Lord Shiro Shayzien
atoms.detail: Speak to Lord Shiro Shayzien upstairs in the War Tent.

atoms.label: Travel to Mount Quidamortem's summit
atoms.detail: Travel to the summit of Mount Quidamortem via the Lovakengj Minecart Network, Xeric's Talisman (Xeric's Honour, if unlocked), or fairy ring code BLS (if unlocked). Optionally speak to the Mountain Guide here to unlock quick travel for later trips.

atoms.label: Speak to Historian Duffy
atoms.detail: Speak to Historian Duffy, found near the water symbol on the minimap.

atoms.label: Enter the mountain crevice
atoms.detail: Use a rope on the crevice on the western side of the mountain and enter it, then go around to the south — not through the middle (near the exclamation-mark symbol on the minimap).

atoms.label: Mine the boulder
atoms.detail: Mine the boulder blocking the passage.

atoms.label: Push the boulder aside
atoms.detail: Push the mined boulder out of the way.

atoms.label: Defeat the Corrupt Lizardman
atoms.detail: Attempt to pass the magic gate; a Corrupt Lizardman blocks it — kill it.

atoms.label: Inspect the Unstable Altar
atoms.detail: Pass through the magic gate and inspect the Unstable Altar.

atoms.label: Hop worlds to leave the cave
atoms.detail: Once the dialogue finishes, hop worlds to quickly leave the cave.

atoms.label: Speak to Duffy again
atoms.detail: Speak to Historian Duffy again on the mountain.

atoms.label: Return to the unstable altar
atoms.detail: Go back into the cave to the unstable altar.

atoms.label: Speak to both Duffy and Gnosi
atoms.detail: Speak to Duffy, then to Gnosi, near the unstable altar — make sure to talk to BOTH of them.

atoms.label: Report to Lord Shiro Shayzien
atoms.detail: Speak to Lord Shiro Shayzien back at the War Tent (fairy ring code DJR or the Lovakengj Minecart Network), or speak to the Mountain Guide if quick travel was unlocked earlier.

atoms.label: Check Phileas's empty house
atoms.detail: Return to Phileas's house and walk inside to trigger dialogue — he won't physically be there.

atoms.label: Finish the investigation
atoms.detail: Return to Lord Shiro Shayzien once more to complete the quest.

checkpoints.label: Report back at the War Tent

steps.detail: Required to train Herblore. Short quest, no stat requirements.

mapMarkers.label: Kaqemeex, stone circle N of Taverley (start)

hints.note: start option
Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

media.caption: Druid's stone circle, north of Taverley: Kaqemeex (quest-druidic-ritual start)
Player beside Kaqemeex; talk to him to unlock Herblore training

atoms.label: Talk to Kaqemeex (start Druidic Ritual)
atoms.detail: Kaqemeex stands in the stone circle north of Taverley; agree to help the druids reclaim the ritual site.

atoms.label: Talk to Sanfew in the Taverley Herb shop
atoms.detail: Sanfew is upstairs in Taverley's Herb shop; he explains the meat-enchanting ritual and asks for four raw meats.

atoms.label: Kill a bear for raw bear meat
atoms.detail: Gather-not-GE alternative to the wiki's own 'these meats can all be bought on the Grand Exchange' shortcut (flagged GE-violation): kill a bear near the building just west of Ice Mountain.

atoms.label: Kill a chicken for raw chicken
atoms.detail: Gather-not-GE alternative to the wiki's own GE-buy shortcut (flagged GE-violation): kill a chicken at the farm just south-east of Falador.

atoms.label: Kill a cow for raw beef
atoms.detail: Gather-not-GE alternative to the wiki's own GE-buy shortcut (flagged GE-violation): the same farm south-east of Falador also has a cow.

atoms.label: Kill a giant rat for raw rat meat
atoms.detail: Gather-not-GE alternative to the wiki's own GE-buy shortcut (flagged GE-violation): kill the giant rat near the graveyard just north of the church below Port Sarim.

atoms.label: Go to the Cauldron in Taverley Dungeon
atoms.detail: Enter Taverley Dungeon and head to the Cauldron room.

atoms.label: Enchant the four meats on the Cauldron
atoms.detail: Use each of the four raw meats (bear, rat, beef, chicken) on the Cauldron one at a time to enchant them. The Quick guide doesn't name the resulting item(s) distinctly from the raw meats -- treated as a 1:1 transform per meat, flagged "??" on exact post-enchant item naming.

atoms.label: Return the enchanted meats to Sanfew
atoms.detail: Bring the four enchanted meats back to Sanfew in the Taverley Herb shop.

atoms.label: Return to Kaqemeex to finish the quest
atoms.detail: Talk to Kaqemeex at the stone circle north of Taverley to complete Druidic Ritual and unlock Herblore training.

steps.detail: Lumbridge Cook needs ingredients for the Duke's birthday party. Starter quest, no reqs.

hints.note: start: agree to help with the Duke's birthday cake
Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

media.caption: Lumbridge Castle kitchen: the Cook (quest-cooks-assistant start)
Player beside the Cook; he needs ingredients for the Duke's birthday cake

atoms.label: Talk to the Cook (start Cook's Assistant)
atoms.detail: The Cook is in Lumbridge Castle's kitchen, panicking over the Duke's birthday cake. Agree to fetch a bucket of milk, an egg, and a pot of flour for him.

atoms.label: Grab an empty bucket in the castle cellar
atoms.detail: An empty bucket sits in the Lumbridge Castle cellar (basement); pick it up before heading out to the cow field.

atoms.label: Milk a dairy cow in the Lumbridge cow field
atoms.detail: Gather-not-GE alternative to the wiki's own 'buy from the Grand Exchange' shortcut (flagged GE-violation): use the bucket on a dairy cow in the Lumbridge cow field, just south of the castle.

atoms.label: Pick up an egg at the chicken coop
atoms.detail: Gather-not-GE alternative to the wiki's own 'buy from the Grand Exchange' shortcut (flagged GE-violation): an egg spawns at the chicken coop north-west of the cow field; pick one up.

atoms.label: Grab an empty pot from the Cook
atoms.detail: A spare empty pot sits by the Cook in Lumbridge Castle's kitchen.

atoms.label: Pick grain from the wheat field by Mill Lane Mill
atoms.detail: Grain grows in the wheat field just outside Mill Lane Mill, next to the cow pens.

atoms.label: Mill the grain into a pot of flour
atoms.detail: Climb to the top floor of Mill Lane Mill, use the grain on the hopper, then pull the lever. Climb back down and take the flour from the flour bin using the empty pot.

atoms.label: Deliver the milk, egg, and flour to the Cook
atoms.detail: Bring the bucket of milk, egg, and pot of flour to the Cook in Lumbridge Castle's kitchen and hand them over to finish the quest.

checkpoints.label: Gather the three ingredients (milk, egg, flour)

steps.detail: Unlocks Fossil Island. Required to mine volcanic ash for ultracompost.

hints.note: start: offer to help finish the barge

media.caption: Varrock Museum: Curator Haig Halen (quest-bone-voyage start)
Player beside the Curator; sign up for the fossil-hunting barge voyage

atoms.label: Talk to Curator Haig Halen
atoms.detail: Speak to Curator Haig Halen in the Varrock Museum and agree to help finish the barge.

atoms.label: Travel to the canal barge
atoms.detail: Go to the canal barge north of the Digsite.

atoms.label: Talk to the barge foreman
atoms.detail: Talk to the barge foreman at the canal barge.

atoms.label: Get the sawmill proposal
atoms.detail: Talk to the sawmill operator in the Lumber Yard, explaining you're there on behalf of the museum archaeological team.

atoms.label: Deliver the sawmill proposal
atoms.detail: Bring the sawmill proposal to the sawmill operator in the Woodcutting Guild; if entry requirements aren't met, simply try opening the guild's eastern gate to satisfy this step.

atoms.label: Deliver the signed agreement
atoms.detail: Bring the sawmill agreement back to the Lumber Yard's sawmill operator.

atoms.label: Report back to the barge foreman
atoms.detail: Talk to the barge foreman again.

atoms.label: Board the barge
atoms.detail: Right-click the Embark option on the barge guard to board the barge.

atoms.label: Talk to the Lead Navigator
atoms.detail: Talk to the Lead Navigator aboard the barge.

atoms.label: Travel to the Rusty Anchor Inn
atoms.detail: Travel to the Rusty Anchor Inn in Port Sarim.

atoms.label: Ask Jack Seagull about cursed voyages
atoms.detail: Talk to Jack Seagull in the Rusty Anchor Inn about cursed voyages.

atoms.label: Re-board the barge
atoms.detail: Return to the northern part of the Digsite and board the boat again through the barge guard.

atoms.label: Talk to the Lead Navigator again
atoms.detail: Talk to the Lead Navigator once more.

atoms.label: Get the Bone charm from the Odd Old Man
atoms.detail: Deboard, walk west then north then east to the limestone mine, and speak to the Odd Old Man about lucky charms and the cursed voyage; he gives a Bone charm.

atoms.label: Obtain 2 bottles of vodka
atoms.detail: Obtain 2 bottles of vodka -- the walkthrough does not state a sourcing method here ("??"); no Grand Exchange purchase is used regardless.

atoms.label: Gather a marrentill herb
atoms.detail: Gather a marrentill herb (Herblore farming patch or a common secondary monster drop).

atoms.label: Fill a vial with water
atoms.detail: Fill an empty vial with water from any water source.

atoms.label: Make a Marrentill potion (unf)
atoms.detail: Use the marrentill herb on the vial of water to produce a Marrentill potion (unf).

atoms.label: Bring the vodka and potion to the Apothecary
atoms.detail: Bring the 2 vodka bottles and the Marrentill potion (unf) to the Apothecary in Varrock; select 'Talk about something else' then 'Talk about Bone Voyage'.

atoms.label: Receive the potion of sealegs
atoms.detail: Speak to the Apothecary again, selecting the same chat options as before, to receive the potion of sealegs.

atoms.label: Deliver the potion of sealegs
atoms.detail: Return to the Lead Navigator and give him the potion of sealegs.

atoms.label: Hand over the bone charm and depart
atoms.detail: Speak to the Junior Navigator, give him the bone charm, and confirm you're ready to go.

atoms.label: Steer the barge to Fossil Island
atoms.detail: Click the arrow opposite whichever way the top arrow tilts to correct course, and keep the sails as large as possible in a straight line for steadier travel, until the progress bar fills and you arrive at Fossil Island.

steps.detail: No requirements. Fred wants 20 balls of wool. Fastest route is simply buying 20 balls of wool (tradeable) and handing them in; the 'intended' route -- shear sheep in the pen with shears, spin the wool at a spinning wheel -- is optional and grants +50 bonus Crafting xp over just buying. Same opener tier as Cook's Assistant, designed to walk new players through Lumbridge.

req_items.name: Balls of wool
req_items.note: unnoted; shears needed instead if shearing/spinning your own

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Talk to Fred the Farmer north of Lumbridge
atoms.detail: Talk to Fred the Farmer, north of Lumbridge, and ask about a quest; he gives you shears if you don't already have a pair.

atoms.label: Shear 20 wool from the sheep in Fred's pen
atoms.detail: Enter the sheep pen east of Fred's house and shear sheep with the shears for 20 wool. Rams and 'The Thing' cannot be sheared -- leave the pen if one starts attacking you and it will stop targeting you. Ultimate Ironmen may deliver the wool in several smaller trips.

atoms.label: Go to the 1st floor of Lumbridge Castle
atoms.detail: Walk to the 1st floor (UK numbering) of Lumbridge Castle, where the spinning wheel is.

atoms.label: Spin the 20 wool into balls of wool
atoms.detail: Use the spinning wheel in the southern room to spin all 20 wool into 20 un-noted balls of wool.

atoms.label: Hand in the 20 balls of wool to Fred
atoms.detail: Return to Fred the Farmer with all 20 un-noted balls of wool and hand them in to complete the quest.

checkpoints.label: Get shears from Fred the Farmer
Shear the sheep and spin the wool

steps.detail: Novice quest; only real gate is being able to kill or flee a level 13 skeleton. Talk to Father Aereck about the haunted Lumbridge graveyard, head to Father Urhney's hut west of Lumbridge Swamp and talk to him, then go to the small room in the graveyard, equip the Ghostspeak amulet and talk to the coffin-ghost. Go to the Wizards' Tower basement and search the altar for a ghost's skull (a level 13 skeleton spawns but does not need to be killed, just leave), then use the skull on the open coffin to finish. Required for: Animal Magnetism, Curse of the Empty Lord, Ghosts Ahoy, Making History, Nature Spirit, Creature of Fenkenstrain.

media.caption: Lumbridge church: Father Aereck (quest-the-restless-ghost start)
Player beside Father Aereck; ask him about the haunted graveyard to start the quest

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Talk to Father Aereck in the Lumbridge church
atoms.detail: Lumbridge teleport reaches the church directly; ask about a quest to start.

hints.value: I'm looking for a quest! -> Ok, let me help then.

atoms.label: Head to Father Urhney's hut west of Lumbridge Swamp
atoms.detail: Exit the graveyard through its southern gate and continue west of the swamp to reach the hut.

atoms.label: Talk to Father Urhney inside the hut
atoms.detail: Explain that Father Aereck sent you about the graveyard haunting.

hints.value: Father Aereck sent me to talk to you. -> He's got a ghost haunting his graveyard.

the quick guide never spells out a 'receive amulet' bullet -- the Ghostspeak amulet's grant is inferred because the very next step names and equips it, per the never-fabricate-but-infer-from-explicit-next-step precedent

atoms.label: Head to the small room in the Lumbridge graveyard, south of the church
Equip the Ghostspeak amulet
atoms.detail: Needed to understand the coffin ghost's speech.

atoms.label: Click the coffin and talk to the ghost
atoms.detail: The ghost explains it needs its skull back before it can rest.

hints.value: Yep, now tell me what the problem is.

atoms.label: Head to the Wizards' Tower and climb down to the basement
atoms.detail: A Necklace of passage can teleport straight to the Wizards' Tower if owned.

atoms.label: Search the altar for the ghost's skull
atoms.detail: A level 13 skeleton spawns when the altar is searched; it does not need to be fought, just leave the room.

atoms.label: Return to the graveyard with the ghost's skull
Use the ghost's skull on the open coffin
atoms.detail: Completes the quest.

checkpoints.label: Talk to Father Aereck and head to Father Urhney's hut
Get the Ghostspeak amulet and question the graveyard ghost
Retrieve the ghost's skull and finish the quest

steps.detail: No requirements, tutorial-tier treasure hunt intro quest. Talk to Veos in the Sheared Ram pub, Lumbridge, to start. Dig at 4 marked spots in sequence: by Bob's Brilliant Axes (west of the plant against the wall), behind Lumbridge Castle near the kitchen door, north-west of the Draynor Village jail by the wheat farm, and in the pig pen next to Martin the Master Gardener. Use the resulting ancient casket on Veos at the Port Sarim docks to finish.

req_items.name: Spade
req_items.note: Eastfloor spade does not work for this quest

atoms.label: Talk to Veos in The Sheared Ram, Lumbridge
atoms.detail: Ask Veos about a quest to begin X Marks the Spot.

hints.value: If Great Kourend has already been visited, the opening options differ: "1 Can I help?" then "1 Sounds good, what do I do?"

atoms.label: Dig at the first clue spot by Bob's Brilliant Axes
atoms.detail: Dig right under the north-westernmost window of the small room attached to Bob's Brilliant Axes, one tile west of the plant against the wall.

atoms.label: Dig at the second clue spot behind Lumbridge Castle
atoms.detail: Dig just outside the kitchen door on the castle's south-west side, one tile south-west of the large crate.

atoms.label: Travel to Draynor Village
atoms.detail: Walk to Draynor Village, or teleport there with an Amulet of glory.

atoms.label: Dig at the third clue spot near the Draynor jail
atoms.detail: Dig north-west of the Draynor Village jail, by the wheat farm, four tiles north of the bush near Leela.

atoms.label: Dig at the fourth clue spot in the pig pen
atoms.detail: Dig in the pig pen next to Martin the Master Gardener to unearth the ancient casket.

atoms.label: Use the ancient casket on Veos at the Port Sarim docks
atoms.detail: Find Veos at the Port Sarim docks, south of the Rusty Anchor Inn, and use the ancient casket on him to complete the quest.

steps.detail: Track down a crop-raiding beast for farmer Sarah at the Falador farm: follow a trail of stolen vegetables to a hole near Draynor Manor, investigate a skeleton underground, get reinforced goggles from Spria, then kill the level-37 Sourhog and bring its foot back as proof.

req_items.name: knife or slash weapon
req_items.note: not whip/tentacle/halberd/claws; obtainable during the quest

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Check the noticeboard behind the Wine Stall
atoms.detail: The board sits on the wall behind the Wine Stall in Draynor Village market.

atoms.label: Talk to Sarah about the bounty
atoms.detail: Sarah works near the loom at South Falador Farm, north of Port Sarim; trading her also completes an easy Falador Diary task.

atoms.label: Follow the vegetable trail to the strange hole
atoms.detail: From the crossroads north of Draynor Village and south of Draynor Manor, follow scattered cabbages and carrots north-east to the hole.

atoms.label: Use a rope on the hole and climb down
atoms.detail: A rope opens the way down into the cave.

atoms.label: Investigate the skeleton at the cave's end
atoms.detail: A cutscene plays after investigating the skeleton.

atoms.label: Talk to Spria for reinforced goggles
atoms.detail: Spria appears after the cutscene and hands over protective goggles.

atoms.label: Equip the reinforced goggles
atoms.detail: Wear the goggles before facing the Sourhog.

atoms.label: Kill the level 37 Sourhog back down the hole
atoms.detail: Sourhog resists slash and magic damage; bring a non-slash, non-whip/claw weapon or other suitable combat gear.

atoms.label: Remove the foot from the dead Sourhog
atoms.detail: Take the foot as proof of the kill.

atoms.label: Return the Sourhog's foot to Sarah
atoms.detail: Hand the foot over to claim the bounty.

atoms.label: Report back to Spria to finish
atoms.detail: Talk to Spria in Draynor Village to complete the quest.

steps.detail: Requires the ability to defeat level 45+ enemies (no formal skill/quest gate). Instanced combat gauntlet down a rift near the Digsite: fill a rage bar killing angry unicorns/bears/rats/goblins with themed weapons in room 1, hunt 5-6 fear reapers hiding in holes in room 2, tell 6 confusion beasts apart from their fakes (fakes vanish after 8 hits, give free combat xp) in room 3, kill hopeless creatures 3 times each in room 4, then defeat all 3 heads of Tolna to finish. Progress resets on logout mid-room; re-entering the rift post-quest also completes a Varrock Medium diary task.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Talk to Launa by the rift
atoms.detail: Speak with Launa, found north-east of Varrock near the Digsite by the rift.

hints.value: Would you like me to go down and look for your husband and son?

atoms.label: Enter the rift with a rope
atoms.detail: Enter the rift using a rope to begin the instanced dungeon; do not click outside the dialogue boxes during the entry cutscene or you will return to the surface.

atoms.label: Fill the rage bar using the four themed weapons
atoms.detail: Use the anger sword on angry unicorns, the anger spear on angry bears, the anger mace on angry giant rats, and the anger battleaxe on angry goblins from the room's rack until the rage bar is full (logging out resets the bar); filling it grants 40 Attack experience.

atoms.label: Head east to the next cave
atoms.detail: Move east into the next chamber after the rage-bar cutscene.

atoms.label: Search holes clockwise for fear reapers
atoms.detail: Look inside the dark holes going clockwise (a searched hole cannot be searched again) and kill 5 or 6 fear reapers to trigger the room's cutscene.

atoms.label: Enter the black hole to the west
atoms.detail: Move west through the black hole once enough fear reapers are defeated.

atoms.label: Defeat confusion beasts until one door remains
atoms.detail: Attack confusion beasts to tell the real one (takes damage) from the fakes (which give combat xp on missed hits and vanish after 8 hits); disengage by running to the opposite end of the room if needed, and repeat until only one door remains.

atoms.label: Enter the remaining door
atoms.detail: Move through the one door left open after the confusion beasts are cleared.

atoms.label: Kill every hopeless creature three times each
atoms.detail: Defeat each hopeless creature in the room three times.

atoms.label: Cross the bridge toward Tolna
atoms.detail: Cross the bridge to prepare for the fight against Tolna's three level-46 heads.

atoms.label: Kill Tolna's three heads
atoms.detail: Defeat all three of Tolna's heads; using Protect from Missiles (ranged) or Protect from Melee (melee, if underlevelled) avoids damage. The final cutscene/dialogue must be finished before attacking or you are returned to the entrance.

atoms.label: Talk to Tolna
atoms.detail: Speak with Tolna after defeating his three heads.

atoms.label: Talk to Tolna again on the surface
atoms.detail: Return to the surface and speak with Tolna once more to finish the quest; re-entering the rift afterward completes a Varrock Medium Achievement Diary task.

steps.detail: Novice quest, opens the troll questline. Talk to Denulth about the troll raids on Burthorpe; ask Eohric on the 1st floor of the castle then Harold at the Burthorpe bar about the missing guard. Give Harold an Asgarnian ale (a Blurberry special auto-wins the dice bet, otherwise gamble over 60gp repeatedly until he goes bankrupt) for an IOU note with a combination; use the combination on the 5 coloured balls at the castle to unlock a door. Talk to Saba in the cave north-west of Burthorpe about another route up the plateau; talk to Tenzing, then Dunstan for a certificate, and exchange the certificate + climbing boots + iron bar for spiked boots. Give Tenzing 10 unnoted bread + 10 unnoted trout + the spiked boots for a map, walk the back route around the mountain (ignore the Death Plateau warning message on the main path), and talk to Denulth with the combination to finish. Required for: Troll Stronghold.

req_items.name: coins
req_items.note: bring 1000 to skip the dice-gamble grind

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Talk to Denulth in the Burthorpe camp
atoms.detail: Ask Denulth for a quest to begin Death Plateau.

atoms.label: Ask Eohric about last night's guard, 1st floor of castle
atoms.detail: Ask Eohric on the castle's first floor about the guard who was on duty the previous night.

atoms.label: Talk to Harold upstairs in the Burthorpe bar
atoms.detail: Confront Harold about being the guard on duty last night.

atoms.label: Talk to Eohric again
atoms.detail: Report back to Eohric.

atoms.label: Go back to Harold
atoms.detail: Return to Harold in the bar.

atoms.label: Give Harold an Asgarnian ale
atoms.detail: Hand Harold an Asgarnian ale; a Blurberry special auto-wins the following gamble instead of leaving it to chance.

atoms.label: Gamble with Harold (over 60gp) until he is bankrupt
atoms.detail: Bet more than 60 gold repeatedly (101 bankrupts him immediately) until Harold has no money left.

hints.value: no gamble primitive in the closed verb enum; mapped to gather

atoms.label: Read the IOU for the door combination
atoms.detail: Once bankrupt, Harold gives an IOU; read it to learn the combination.

atoms.label: Set the 5 coloured balls at the castle to the combination
atoms.detail: Use the combination to place the 5 coloured balls correctly, unlocking the storage room door; keep the note for later.

atoms.label: Head north-west of Burthorpe to Saba's cave
atoms.detail: Enter the cave north-west of Burthorpe.

atoms.label: Ask Saba about another way up Death Plateau
atoms.detail: Ask Saba whether there is an alternate route up the plateau.

atoms.label: Leave the cave and go north-west then south at the fork
atoms.detail: Exit the cave and follow the path north-west, then south at the fork.

atoms.label: Talk to Tenzing the Sherpa
atoms.detail: Agree to fetch items for Tenzing.

atoms.label: Talk to Dunstan by the anvil in north-east Burthorpe
atoms.detail: Speak with Dunstan near the anvil.

atoms.label: Talk to Denulth for a certificate
atoms.detail: Return to Denulth where the quest started to receive a certificate.

atoms.label: Exchange boots, certificate and iron bar for spiked boots
atoms.detail: Give Dunstan the climbing boots, certificate, and an iron bar to receive spiked boots (certificate only if already owned).

atoms.label: Give Tenzing 10 bread, 10 trout and spiked boots for the map
atoms.detail: Hand Tenzing 10 unnoted loaves of bread, 10 unnoted cooked trout, and the spiked boots all at once to get the map.

atoms.label: Follow the back route around the mountain
atoms.detail: Climb the stile north of Tenzing's house and follow the path east past the second mountain goat until 'I think this is far enough' appears (not the Death Plateau warning, which is safe to ignore).

atoms.label: Talk to Denulth with the combination to finish
atoms.detail: Report to Denulth with the combination in hand to complete the quest; it can be re-obtained free from Harold if lost.

steps.detail: No requirements. Doric wants an errand run for anvil access: buy or mine (with the bronze pickaxe he gives you) 2 iron ore + 4 copper ore + 6 clay, all un-noted, from the Dwarven Mine south of Ice Mountain just east of the start point, then hand them to Doric. One of the short prerequisite quests feeding into Devious Minds and the Falador diary.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Speak to Doric to start the quest
atoms.detail: Talk to Doric at his house just outside Taverley's east gate, north of Falador; he gives you a bronze pickaxe.

atoms.label: Mine iron ore south-east of the mine ladder
atoms.detail: Mine 2 un-noted iron ore at the spot just south-east of the ladder down into the Dwarven Mine, south of Ice Mountain (east of the quest start).

atoms.label: Mine copper ore south-east of the mine ladder
atoms.detail: Mine 4 un-noted copper ore at the same spot south-east of the ladder; watch for aggressive Scorpions/King Scorpions at low combat.

atoms.label: Mine clay near the agility shortcut
atoms.detail: Mine 6 un-noted clay south of the iron/copper spot, near the Agility shortcut.

atoms.label: Hand the materials to Doric to finish
atoms.detail: Talk to Doric with the un-noted 2 iron ore, 4 copper ore and 6 clay in your inventory to complete the quest.

hints.value: 1I wanted to use your anvils.|1Yes, I will get you the materials.

checkpoints.label: Mine ore and clay, then deliver

steps.detail: Classic quest, gates the Rune platebody and a huge amount of mid-game content. Get Melzar's, Thalzar's, and Lozar's map pieces to Crandor in any order: Thalzar's requires using silk+lobster pot+unfired bowl+wizard's mind bomb on a magic door in the Dwarven Mine; Melzar's requires clearing his eponymous maze in Rimmington, killing a sequence of monsters (zombie rat, ghost, skeleton, zombies, Melzar the Mad, then a level-82 lesser demon) to collect keys; Lozar's is bought from or telegrabbed off Wormbrain in Port Sarim's jail. Combine the three pieces, buy/repair a boat with 3 planks+90 steel nails+2,000 coins from Klarense, sail to Crandor via Ned in Draynor Village, and kill the level-83 dragon Elvarg with an anti-dragon shield equipped (antifire potion recommended). Report back to Oziach in Edgeville to finish.

req_items.name: combat option for Elvarg (magic/ranged weapon + law+air rune) or 10,000 coins

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Ask the Guildmaster for a quest
atoms.detail: Speak to the Guildmaster in the Champions' Guild to start the quest.

atoms.label: Learn about the dragon from Oziach
atoms.detail: Speak to Oziach in Edgeville; he explains the rune platebody and the dragon Elvarg.

atoms.label: Get the maze key and map-piece leads
atoms.detail: Return to the Guildmaster; get the Melzar's Maze key and ask where all three map pieces and the right ship are.

atoms.label: Get an anti-dragon shield if you lack one
atoms.detail: Optional: if you don't already own an anti-dragon shield, ask Duke Horacio on the 1st floor of Lumbridge Castle for one.

atoms.label: Ask the Oracle about Thalzar's map piece
atoms.detail: Speak to the Oracle on Ice Mountain about the map piece, after already asking the Guildmaster where it is.

atoms.label: Open the magic door in the Dwarven Mine
atoms.detail: In the Dwarven Mine's north-east side, use silk, a lobster pot, an unfired bowl, then the wizard's mind bomb on the magic door; it opens automatically.

atoms.label: Search the chest for Thalzar's map piece
atoms.detail: Open and search the chest beyond the magic door to collect the map piece; re-entry does not require reusing the items.

atoms.label: Enter Melzar's Maze
atoms.detail: With the maze key in hand, travel to Rimmington and enter Melzar's Maze.

atoms.label: Kill the long-tailed zombie rat for a key
atoms.detail: Kill the small zombie rat with the long tail, take its key, and go through the north-western door.

atoms.label: Kill the hooded ghost for a key
atoms.detail: Kill the ghost wearing a hood and tube top (no cape), take its key, go through the second door from the north on the east wall.

atoms.label: Kill the round-shield skeleton for a key
atoms.detail: Kill the skeleton carrying the round shield, take its key, go through the westmost door on the south wall and descend the ladder three times.

atoms.label: Kill zombies until a key drops
atoms.detail: Kill the maze zombies for a key, then go through the blue door. Exact number of zombies to defeat is unconfirmed.

atoms.label: Kill Melzar the Mad for a key
atoms.detail: Kill Melzar the Mad, take his key, and go through the purple (magenta) door.

atoms.label: Kill the level-82 lesser demon for a key
atoms.detail: Kill the level-82 lesser demon (can be safespotted by luring it to the magenta door), take its key, and go through the green door.

atoms.label: Search the maze's final chest
atoms.detail: Open and search the chest to collect Melzar's map piece.

atoms.label: Go to the Port Sarim jail
atoms.detail: Travel to the jail in Port Sarim to find Wormbrain.

atoms.label: Obtain Lozar's map piece from Wormbrain
atoms.detail: Kill Wormbrain with a ranged/magic/halberd attack and telegrab the dropped map piece, or pay him instead; ask the Guildmaster first for his location.

hints.value: pay Wormbrain 10,000 coins instead of killing him

atoms.label: Combine the three map pieces
atoms.detail: Use the three collected map pieces on each other to assemble the full Crandor map.

atoms.label: Ask the Guildmaster about a ship and captain
atoms.detail: Return to the Guildmaster and ask where to find a ship and its captain.

atoms.label: Withdraw boat-repair supplies
atoms.detail: Withdraw a hammer, 3 planks, 90 steel nails, and 2,000 coins from the bank.

atoms.label: Buy the ship from Klarense
atoms.detail: Speak to Klarense at the southern docks of Port Sarim near the Void Outpost boat and pay 2,000 coins for the ship.

atoms.label: Repair the hole in the ship
atoms.detail: Board the ship, climb down the ladder, and repair the hole three times using the planks, nails, and hammer.

atoms.label: Sail to Crandor with Ned
atoms.detail: Speak to Ned in Draynor Village with the Crandor map in your inventory to sail to Crandor.

atoms.label: Climb the volcano into Elvarg's lair
atoms.detail: Climb to the top of the volcano and enter the hole down to Elvarg's cave.

atoms.label: Equip the anti-dragon shield
atoms.detail: Equip the anti-dragon shield (and drink an antifire potion if carried) before approaching Elvarg.

atoms.label: Open the shortcut wall (optional)
atoms.detail: Optional: before teleporting out, open the wall south of Elvarg's cave to unlock a permanent shortcut through the Karamja volcano dungeon.

hints.value: unlocks a Karamja diary task and a shortcut route back

atoms.label: Kill Elvarg
atoms.detail: Kill the level-83 dragon Elvarg; flinching her from the south-east corner of the cave is a safer but slower approach. Her head is collected automatically as proof.

atoms.label: Report back to Oziach
atoms.detail: Return to Oziach in Edgeville to complete the quest.

checkpoints.label: Start the quest and gear up

steps.detail: Pick a method: Barbarians / Flesh Crawlers and Zombies (Stronghold of Security, Catacomb of Famine) / Chaos druid.

media.caption: Arrive at Barbarian Village (train-defence-40)
Barbarians around the player; these are the kill target

methods.location: Barbarian Village, south of Edgeville
methods.notes: Low-Defence, high fan-in monster used for early Attack/Strength/Defence training around Barbarian Village.

methods.method: Flesh Crawlers and Zombies (Stronghold of Security, Catacomb of Famine)
methods.location: Stronghold of Security, Catacomb of Famine (level 2)
methods.notes: Zombies (levels 30/44/53) and flesh crawlers (levels 28/35/41) share this floor; flesh crawlers also drop herbs and the bottom of the skull sceptre.

methods.location: Edgeville Dungeon, or Chaos Druid Tower
methods.notes: Thieving 46 is only needed for the Chaos Druid Tower variant; the Edgeville Dungeon spot has no requirement but sits in low-level Wilderness.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw Barbarian Village loadout
atoms.detail: Food ??x, best-available weapon/armour equipped.

atoms.label: Go to Barbarian Village
atoms.detail: Barbarians (combat 8-17) spawn throughout the village.

atoms.label: Kill Barbarians until Defence 40
atoms.detail: Low-level, non-aggressive village NPCs; minimal food needed at this stage.

atoms.label: Bank: deposit Barbarian loot
atoms.detail: Deposit bones and coins.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Flesh Crawlers and Zombies (Stronghold of Security, Catacomb of Famine) / Moss giant / Wilderness Slayer.

methods.method: Flesh Crawlers and Zombies (Stronghold of Security, Catacomb of Famine)
methods.location: Stronghold of Security, Catacomb of Famine (level 2)
methods.notes: Zombies (levels 30/44/53) and flesh crawlers (levels 28/35/41) share this floor; flesh crawlers also drop herbs and the bottom of the skull sceptre.

methods.location: Catacombs of Kourend, Varrock Sewers, or west of the Fishing Guild
methods.notes: Fair Hitpoints for its level with a chance at a Mossy key (Bryophyta access); hits hard on low-Defence/low-armour players.

methods.location: Wilderness
methods.notes: Slayer helm accuracy/damage bonuses make Wilderness Slayer assignments good melee xp while also banking Slayer points quickly; player-vs-player danger applies.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw Stronghold loadout
atoms.detail: Food ??x; no special items required beyond answering the security-question doors.

atoms.label: Go to Stronghold of Security, Catacomb of Famine
atoms.detail: Enter via the Barbarian Village hole, answer the security-question doors, descend to the Catacomb of Famine.

atoms.label: Kill Flesh Crawlers until Defence 60
atoms.detail: Near-AFK at this stat range; claim coin rewards on each floor while passing.

hints.note: Let aggro stack; re-aggro resets on floor exit/re-entry.

atoms.label: Alt: assigned Slayer tasks until Defence 60
atoms.detail: General combat-task progress toward Defence 60 (task source/assigner ??) -- vague alternative to the Stronghold route, kept per the coarse row's own 'Flesh Crawlers or Slayer tasks' detail.

hints.note: Task rolls vary; not concretely locatable without a specific assigner.

atoms.label: Bank: deposit Stronghold loot
atoms.detail: Deposit runes, coins, and ashes.

steps.detail: Short novice quest that unlocks the dwarf multicannon, one of the most impactful early combat-XP tools in the game. Repair 6 bent railings on the west/south fence for Lawgof with a hammer, retrieve dwarf remains from a tower, then find the goblin Lollk hiding in a crate deep in a cave south-east of the Fishing Guild. Reassemble a broken multicannon in the correct tool order (hook on spring, pliers on safety switch, tooth tool on gear), then travel to the Dwarven Black Guard camp to get Nulodion's notes and a cannonball mould before returning to Lawgof to finish.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Speak to Captain Lawgof to start
atoms.detail: Talk to Captain Lawgof by the Coal truck mining site west of Seers' Village (fairy ring code ALS then run east through the loose railing, or skills necklace to Fishing Guild and run west/north).

atoms.label: Take a hammer if needed
atoms.detail: (Optional, if you don't already have one) pick up the hammer in the house to the east.

atoms.label: Repair the six bent railings
atoms.detail: Use the hammer to fix the six bent railings on the west and south sides of the fence.

atoms.label: Report the fence repair
atoms.detail: Talk to Captain Lawgof after fixing the railings.

atoms.label: Walk to the tower south of the gate
atoms.detail: Go to the tower south of the gate.

atoms.label: Take the dwarf remains
atoms.detail: Climb the two ladders inside the tower and take the dwarf remains.

atoms.label: Report the dwarf remains
atoms.detail: Talk to Captain Lawgof after retrieving the dwarf remains.

atoms.label: Walk to the cave south-east of the Fishing Guild
atoms.detail: Run south (or teleport to the Fishing Guild) and enter the cave at its south-east corner.

atoms.label: Find Lollk hiding in a crate
atoms.detail: In the cave, go as far north-west as possible; in the north-westernmost room search the northernmost crate by the east wall to find Lollk. The conversation does not need to be finished.

atoms.label: Report finding Lollk, receive the toolkit
atoms.detail: Talk to Captain Lawgof after finding Lollk; he hands over the cannon toolkit.

hints.value: 1Okay, I'll see what I can do.

atoms.label: Fit the hook tool on the spring
atoms.detail: Use the hook tool (right) on the broken multicannon's spring -- first of the fixed assembly order.

atoms.label: Fit the pliers on the safety switch
atoms.detail: Use the pliers (middle) on the safety switch at the bottom -- second in the assembly order.

atoms.label: Fit the tooth tool on the gear
atoms.detail: Use the tooth tool (left) on the gear at the bottom of the hammer -- third and final in the assembly order.

atoms.label: Hand back the reassembled cannon
atoms.detail: Talk to Captain Lawgof; this conversation must be completely finished.

atoms.label: Walk to the Dwarven Black Guard camp
atoms.detail: Go to the Dwarven Black Guard camp north-east of Falador / south of Ice Mountain / west of Barbarian Village.

atoms.label: Get Nulodion's notes
atoms.detail: Talk to Nulodion, in the house on the west side of the mining camp.

atoms.label: Deliver notes and mould to finish
atoms.detail: Return to Captain Lawgof with Nulodion's notes and your cannonball mould to complete the quest.

hints.note: acquisition point for the cannonball mould is never spelled out in the fetched Quick-guide text

checkpoints.label: Start the quest with Captain Lawgof
Repair the fence and retrieve the cannon parts

steps.detail: Third quest in the Mahjarrat series. Lady Servil's family has been kidnapped by General Khazard to fight in his arena. Get Khazard armour from a chest in a house south of Ardougne Monastery and wear it to be let into the prison. Free Sammy Servil after buying Khali brew (5 coins) from the Khazard barman to get the Head Guard drunk and steal his cell keys. Fight through a scripted sequence in the arena: level 63 Khazard ogre, level 44 Khazard scorpion, level 137 Bouncer (hellhound) -- all safespottable behind a skeleton, or use Protect from Melee. The optional level 142 General Khazard fight can be skipped via the door's quick-escape. Return to Lady Servil to finish.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Talk to Lady Servil outside the Tree Gnome Village maze
atoms.detail: She explains her family's kidnapping by General Khazard and starts the quest.

atoms.label: Search the chest in the north-easternmost house for Khazard armour
atoms.detail: The house sits near the Tree Gnome Village maze's north-east corner.

atoms.label: Equip the Khazard armour
atoms.detail: It's required to be let into the prison; keep it on the whole time you're inside.

atoms.label: Head south into the Khazard prison
atoms.detail: Enter through the outer door while wearing the Khazard armour.

atoms.label: Talk to the Head Guard in the south jail room
atoms.detail: He hints he'd fall asleep for the right drink, opening up a barman dialogue option.

atoms.label: Go outside to the bar west of the jail
atoms.detail: The Khazard barman there sells the drink you need.

atoms.label: Buy a Khali brew from the Khazard barman
atoms.detail: Only available once you've spoken to the right guard; costs 5 coins.

hints.value: I'd like a Khali brew please.

atoms.label: Give the Khali brew to the Head Guard
atoms.detail: He drinks it and falls asleep, dropping the cell keys.

atoms.label: Unlock Sammy's cell with the keys
atoms.detail: His is the north-easternmost cell; freeing him kicks off the arena fight sequence.

hints.value: Between fights you can leave to resupply without needing to re-equip the Khazard armour.

atoms.label: Kill the Khazard Ogre (level 63)
atoms.detail: Safespottable behind a skeleton, or tank it with Protect from Melee.

atoms.label: Talk to cellmate Hengrad to begin the next fight
atoms.detail: You're re-jailed with him between each arena opponent.

atoms.label: Kill the Khazard scorpion (level 44)
atoms.detail: Safespottable like the ogre.

atoms.label: Kill Bouncer, a level 137 hellhound
atoms.detail: Once this fight starts, engaging General Khazard next becomes a point of no return for arena re-entry.

atoms.label: Kill General Khazard (level 142), or flee through the south-east door
atoms.detail: Optional fight; escaping through the door or running north until he loses aggression skips it.

hints.value: Purely optional — the quest completes either way.

atoms.label: Return to Lady Servil to finish the quest
atoms.detail: Reporting back completes Fight Arena.

checkpoints.label: Get the Khazard armour and reach the prison
Free Sammy Servil from his cell
Fight through the scripted arena sequence

mapMarkers.label: Stronghold of Security, Catacomb of Famine

Trout/salmon spot + range are adjacent — restock food between kill sets.

steps.detail: Enter Stronghold of Security (Barbarian Village hole), answer security-question doors, descend to level 2. Crawlers aggro and hit low — near-AFK, no food cost at these stats. Claim the coin rewards on each floor while passing.

hints.note: Doors ask account-security questions; any correct answer opens.
Let aggro stack; re-aggro resets on floor exit/re-entry.

methods.location: Catacombs of Kourend, Varrock Sewers, or west of the Fishing Guild
methods.notes: Fair Hitpoints for its level with a chance at a Mossy key (Bryophyta access); hits hard on low-Defence/low-armour players.

methods.notes: Thieving 46 is only needed for the Chaos Druid Tower variant; the Edgeville Dungeon spot has no requirement but sits in low-level Wilderness.

atoms.detail: Withdraw: brass key (?? -- source separately if not owned), food ??x, best-available weapon equipped.
