steps.detail: Pick a method: Willow trees / Teak trees.

methods.location: Woodcutting Guild pond, Draynor Village bank, Seers' Village, or Barbarian Outpost
methods.notes: The page's own fastest-xp path actually keeps players on oak until 35 rather than willow, but willow is the well-known casual pick used here for route continuity.

methods.location: Teak-tree areas (e.g. Fossil Island patches)
methods.notes: 34,000 xp/hr is the page's plain no-tick-manipulation rate at level 35; the page calls teak the fastest xp in the game once tick manipulation is added.

atoms.label: Go to the Draynor Village willow trees
atoms.detail: Same bank-adjacent spot as train-woodcutting-30, tile (3088,3255).

atoms.label: Chop willow logs until Woodcutting 45
atoms.detail: Bank or drop logs; this band's stock is a candidate non-GE source for train-firemaking-50's willow-log method noted in that row's own methods[].

steps.detail: Pick a method: Anvil smithing iron/steel items (iron platebody, then steel warhammer) / Giants' Foundry — forge Iron/Steel alloy swords for Kovac / Blast Furnace — smelt non-gold bars for profit.

hints.note: Superheat Item (Magic 43+) gives passive Smithing xp on the same bars — XREF: training:train-magic-43.

methods.method: Anvil smithing iron/steel items (iron platebody, then steel warhammer)
methods.location: West Varrock anvil
methods.notes: Band spans iron platebody (33-39, 104,200 xp/h) and steel warhammer (39-40, 108,500 xp/h) sub-brackets, no single 30-40 figure, so ??. Row's own Superheat/Magic hint is a separate cross-referenced training row, not modeled as its own method here.

methods.method: Giants' Foundry — forge Iron/Steel alloy swords for Kovac
methods.location: Giants' Foundry minigame
methods.notes: 133,920 xp/h is the guide's stated rate for the iron/steel alloy tier, which unlocks at Smithing 30.

methods.method: Blast Furnace — smelt non-gold bars for profit
methods.location: Blast Furnace, Keldagrim
methods.notes: Same as lower bands: slower but usually profitable; the rate table did not expand to literal numbers in the fetched wikitext.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw hammer + banked iron ore/coal
atoms.detail: Withdraw a hammer and banked iron ore + coal (own-mined, non-GE).

atoms.label: Mine iron ore and coal (non-GE)
atoms.detail: 1 iron ore + 2 coal per steel bar (Steel bar's own Recipe, standard furnace). Site not pinned by the route fixture (??); shares the same ore/coal demand as train-magic-43's Superheat loop earlier in this segment.

atoms.label: Smith steel items at an anvil until Smithing 40
atoms.detail: West Varrock anvil, per the route's own method. Smelt bars into an item (e.g. platebody, then warhammer) at the anvil in one bank cycle.

hints.note: Superheat Item (Magic 43+) gives passive Smithing xp on the same bars -- xref: training:train-magic-43 (same recipe, already in this segment).

steps.detail: Pick a method: Iron ore drop-mining (triangle spots) / Granite 3-tick mining / Motherlode Mine.

methods.location: Any of the 16 three-rock iron triangle sites
methods.notes: Continues the level 15-70 iron route; page's ~45,000-55,000 xp/hr figure is for the whole sub-60 range, not this band specifically.

methods.location: Bandit Camp Quarry or Cape Conch mine
methods.notes: Only unlocks at the very end of this band (level 45); page's own xp table starts at level 45 (87,000 xp/hr with full setup).

methods.location: Motherlode Mine, near the Mining Guild in Falador
methods.notes: Low-effort profitable alternative available from level 30; page only gives an xp/hr figure at level 90, so none is cited for this band.

atoms.label: Equip best available pickaxe
atoms.detail: Equip (or withdraw+equip) the best pickaxe your Mining/Attack level allows.

atoms.label: Drop-mine iron ore until Mining 45
atoms.detail: Any of the 16 three-rock iron triangle sites named by the row's own method; exact site not pinned by the route fixture (??). Banked iron ore here is the same non-GE stock train-smithing-40/train-magic-43 in this segment consume.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Flesh Crawlers and Zombies (Stronghold of Security, Catacomb of Famine) / Moss giant / Chaos druid.

methods.method: Flesh Crawlers and Zombies (Stronghold of Security, Catacomb of Famine)
methods.location: Stronghold of Security, Catacomb of Famine (level 2)
methods.notes: Zombies (levels 30/44/53) and flesh crawlers (levels 28/35/41) share this floor; flesh crawlers also drop herbs and the bottom of the skull sceptre.

methods.location: Catacombs of Kourend, Varrock Sewers, or west of the Fishing Guild
methods.notes: Fair Hitpoints for its level with a chance at a Mossy key (Bryophyta access); hits hard on low-Defence/low-armour players.

methods.location: Edgeville Dungeon, or Chaos Druid Tower
methods.notes: Thieving 46 is only needed for the Chaos Druid Tower variant; the Edgeville Dungeon spot has no requirement but sits in low-level Wilderness.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw Hill Giant loadout
atoms.detail: Withdraw: brass key (?? -- source separately if not owned, unlocks the Edgeville Dungeon door), food ??x, best-available weapon equipped.

atoms.label: Go to Edgeville Dungeon (Hill Giant area)
atoms.detail: Entrance in the house north-east of Barbarian Village, west of the Cooks' Guild; requires a brass key.

atoms.label: Kill Hill Giants until Strength 60
atoms.detail: Aggressive to players below combat 57 (max hit 4); always drops big bones. F2P-crowded -- hop worlds if contested. Alt (str60-site, optional): reuse existing steps.jsonl row ctr-06-stronghold-crawlers per U8 -- its own until=40/40/40 undershoots strength:60, continue past that threshold with the same Flesh Crawlers if still short.

hints.note: Big bones always drop -- bank for the Prayer band (tpb) or bury on the spot.
Edgeville Dungeon is usually crowded on F2P worlds.

atoms.label: Bank: deposit Hill Giant loot
atoms.detail: Deposit big bones, coins, and any weapon/armour drops.

checkpoints.label: Hill Giant / Flesh Crawler routing (pick one)

steps.detail: Pick a method: Cage fishing lobsters / 3-tick fly fishing trout/salmon (continuing to 58).

methods.location: Karamja or Catherby
methods.notes: Matches the row's own label. Confirmed as a real fishing type via the page's Equipment section (lobster pot, non-consumable) but the current Fastest-experience table no longer lists lobster cage fishing as a top method, so no xp/hr figure is stated for it on this page.

methods.location: Barbarian Village (or Prifddinas/Shilo Village spots)

items: swamp tar and a suitable herb for tick manipulation

methods.notes: Page's own section header states fly fishing remains the fastest method up to level 58; nearest stated 3-tick table values are 53,000 xp/hr at level 40 and 61,000 xp/hr at level 50 (no level-58 row exists), left ?? rather than interpolated.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw lobster-pot loadout
atoms.detail: Withdraw a lobster pot and food for the coming combat bands (this band produces cookable food stock).

atoms.label: Go to the Catherby (or Karamja) lobster-cage spot
atoms.detail: Route names 'Karamja or Catherby'; exact cage tile not pinned by the route fixture (??). Catherby town sits at (2834,3445) per the fixture's own later mapMarker (train-fishing-70) -- reused here for approach, cage tile itself still ??.

atoms.label: Cage lobsters until Fishing 58
atoms.detail: Bank trips required (lobster pot is a slower, banked method). Stock the raw lobsters -- next combat bands (Attack/Strength/Defence 60s) need cooked food.

hints.note: Bank/cook the catch -- it pays off as food stock for upcoming combat bands (own-words note, not a formal opp{} weave).

steps.detail: Pick a method: Green dragonhide body / Diamond ring / gem cutting continuation / Water battlestaff.

methods.location: Craft tanned green dragon leather into bodies with a needle and thread (or costume needle) at a bank.
methods.notes: MISMATCH: the row's continuity method names green dragonhide bodies for this 40-54 band, but the page states green d'hide bodies actually require Crafting 63, well above this band's ceiling -- flagged rather than silently re-banded.

methods.location: Continue cutting uncut gems (ruby, then diamond) to sell or use in jewellery.
methods.notes: Ruby unlocks at 34, diamond at 43, both inside this band; page's own text recommends continuing gem cutting up to level 62 before switching methods.

methods.location: Craft water battlestaves per the page's battlestaff table.
methods.notes: Water battlestaff opens at exactly level 54, the band's tail end; the fetched section doesn't name the crafting components so reqs.items is left empty rather than guessed. Page's per-hour figures use a templated crafted-per-hour assumption, not one stated number.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw needle + thread
atoms.detail: Withdraw a needle and thread (or costume needle).

atoms.label: Kill green dragons for dragonhide (non-GE)
atoms.detail: Green dragons (combat level 79, Wilderness) drop Green dragonhide 100% of the time, per the monster's own drop table. Sites named by the wiki: east of the Boneyard Hunter area (3331,3672), west of Dark Warriors' Fortress (2973,3620), north of the Graveyard of Shadows (3137,3707), south of the Lava Maze (3078,3810) -- all deep Wilderness, PK risk.

hints.note: Wilderness PK risk -- hop if contested or under threat.
Same kill also drops Dragon bones (100%) -- xref: training:train-prayer-52 later in this segment can share this exact activity.

atoms.label: Tan green dragonhide into green dragon leather (named vendor, non-GE)
atoms.detail: Ellis, the Al Kharid tanner (tile 3274,3192), tans green dragonhide at the standard 20gp/hide price -- a named NPC service, not the Grand Exchange.

atoms.label: Craft green dragonhide bodies until Crafting 54
atoms.detail: Sew tanned green dragon leather into bodies with a needle and thread at a bank.

steps.detail: Grants access to Ape Atoll and Maniacal Monkey tunnels.

hints.note: start: ask about the missing 10th squad
Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Accept King Narnode's request
atoms.detail: Speak to King Narnode Shareen at the Grand Tree and agree to investigate the missing 10th squad. Keep an inventory slot free or the gnome royal seal drops on the ground.

atoms.label: Glide to Gandius
atoms.detail: Climb up the tree at the Grand Tree and take the gnome glider to Gandius.

atoms.label: Get monkey bones if needed
atoms.detail: If you don't already have monkey bones, kill a monkey nearby for some (if Tai Bwo Wannai Trio is already started, only a monkey corpse drops, which also works).

atoms.label: Enter the Shipyard gate
atoms.detail: Enter the Shipyard gate to the north, carrying the Royal Seal.

atoms.label: Speak with G.L.O. Caranock
atoms.detail: Speak with G.L.O. Caranock in the building south of the Shipyard gate.

atoms.label: Report back to King Narnode
atoms.detail: Return to King Narnode and speak to him. Keep 1 free inventory slot, or Narnode's orders drop to the floor.

atoms.label: Talk through every option with Daero
atoms.detail: Talk to Daero in the bar on the first floor (east side) of the Grand Tree, working through every dialogue option in turn, to be teleported to a new location.

atoms.label: Talk to Daero inside the hangar
atoms.detail: Talk (not "travel") to Daero again, now inside the hangar.

atoms.label: Solve the hangar sliding puzzle
atoms.detail: Click the panel in the south-eastern hangar and solve the sliding puzzle, or alternatively pay Glough back at the Stronghold to solve it for you.

atoms.label: Fly to Crash Island
atoms.detail: Watch the cutscene, then talk to Daero, then to Waydar, to fly to Crash Island.

atoms.label: Speak to Lumdo
atoms.detail: Talk to Lumdo on Crash Island.

atoms.label: Have Waydar convince Lumdo
atoms.detail: Talk (not "travel") to Waydar twice so he convinces Lumdo to follow orders.

atoms.label: Sail to Ape Atoll
atoms.detail: Talk to Lumdo again to travel west to Ape Atoll.

atoms.label: Enter the valley and get arrested
atoms.detail: Follow the red path into the valley on Ape Atoll. You'll be shot and arrested by the guards.

atoms.label: Pick the cell lock and escape
atoms.detail: Watch the guards' patrol pattern, pick the lock on your cell door with a lockpick, and escape. Stay at least 2 squares away from the guards, even while inside the cell.

atoms.label: Talk to Garkor
atoms.detail: Follow the green path and talk to Garkor, completing the full dialogue (skipping it means Zooknock won't give the m'speak amulet later).

atoms.label: Find the monkey dentures
atoms.detail: Follow the blue path to the U-shaped building and enter through the southern door, staying on the dark-brown floor to avoid alerting guards. Search the stacked crates on the light-brown floor for Monkey dentures.

atoms.label: Fall into the cave below
atoms.detail: Heal up first (fall damage applies), then search the south-eastern crate to fall into a cave below.

atoms.label: Find the M'amulet mould
atoms.detail: Search the north-western crates in the cave for a M'amulet mould, then you may teleport away safely.

atoms.label: Mine a gold ore
atoms.detail: Mine a gold ore (Mining 40, pickaxe) at a gold rock. Exact nearest rock to the quest's travel path is unconfirmed this pass ("??") — any gold rock works, this is a generic material, not a quest-location-specific one.

atoms.label: Smelt the gold bar
atoms.detail: Smelt the gold ore into a gold bar at any furnace (Smithing 40, boostable), or cast Superheat Item (Magic 43) to skip the furnace trip.

atoms.label: Shear a sheep for wool
atoms.detail: Shear a sheep with a pair of shears to collect wool — any sheep pen near your bank works (e.g. Lumbridge or Falador).

atoms.label: Spin the wool into a ball
atoms.detail: Spin the wool into a ball of wool on a spinning wheel (Crafting 1).

atoms.label: Descend into the Ape Atoll Dungeon
atoms.detail: Resupply at a bank (good food, prayer/antipoison/stamina potions), return to Ape Atoll via Daero, Waydar, and Lumdo, follow the yellow path west, and climb down the ladder into the Ape Atoll Dungeon.

atoms.label: Fight through to Zooknock
atoms.detail: Run through the dungeon to Zooknock, keeping Protect from Melee active against the level-98 Monkey Zombies and level-142 Skeletons, eating and drinking potions as needed.

atoms.label: Speak to Zooknock
atoms.detail: Talk to Zooknock, the exiled monkey blacksmith.

atoms.label: Have Zooknock forge the enchanted bar
atoms.detail: Use your Monkey dentures, M'amulet mould, and gold bar on Zooknock, then talk to him to receive the Enchanted bar and get your mould back. Do not give him the mould a second time once you already have the bar, or he'll keep it.

atoms.label: Reach the temple with the wall of flames
atoms.detail: Teleport away then return (Daero, Waydar, Lumdo). Follow the red path, get arrested and escape again, then follow the pink path to the temple. Bearded guards can't be walked through — enter the trapdoor, then navigate via the ladder to avoid the gorillas.

atoms.label: Forge the M'speak amulet
atoms.detail: With the M'amulet mould in your inventory, use the Enchanted bar with the wall of flames, then string the result with your ball of wool to create the M'speak amulet.

atoms.label: Head to the Monkey child's house
atoms.detail: Follow the pink path back to the jail, then the cyan path to the Monkey child's house. Watch for the Monkey's Aunt — it's only safe in the garden while she's away.

atoms.label: Convince the monkey child
atoms.detail: Equip the M'speak amulet and talk to the monkey child, then talk to him twice more to trigger "Well I'll be a monkey's uncle!"

atoms.label: Pick 5 bananas
atoms.detail: Pick 5 bananas from the trees in the garden.

atoms.label: Deliver the bananas
atoms.detail: Give the bananas to the monkey child, leave the garden, wait for the aunt to pass through, then talk to him again to receive a Monkey talisman.

atoms.label: Return to Zooknock for the greegree
atoms.detail: Teleport away then return (Daero, Waydar, Lumdo), resupply at a bank, and follow the yellow path down into the Ape Atoll Dungeon again.

atoms.label: Trade for a greegree
atoms.detail: Give Zooknock a Monkey talisman and Monkey bones from a regular monkey (not another type) to receive a Greegree. Be careful not to accidentally bury the bones, and don't leave the Zooknock dialogue early or you'll have to restart the cutscene.

atoms.label: Prepare at the Ardougne Zoo
atoms.detail: Head to the Ardougne Zoo, equip the Karamjan monkey greegree, and talk to the Monkey minder (click through any first-time dialogue).

atoms.label: Bag a monkey
atoms.detail: Equip the M'speak amulet and talk to a nearby monkey so it jumps into your bag. Remove the greegree and speak to the minder again.

atoms.label: Walk back to Ape Atoll on foot
atoms.detail: Travel by foot (walk or run) back to the Grand Tree — do not use any teleportation method — then return to Ape Atoll by talking to Daero, Waydar, and Lumdo.

atoms.label: Reach King Awowogei
atoms.detail: Equip the M'speak amulet and Karamjan monkey greegree — Ape Atoll is no longer hostile while wearing it. Follow the red path through the gates, talk to Garkor, then the nearby Elder Guard, then head to the west coast, climb the hill, cross the bridge, and talk to Kruk.

atoms.label: Speak with King Awowogei
atoms.detail: Talk to Awowogei twice, then talk to the Elder Guard again to be let out.

atoms.label: Receive the 10th squad sigil
atoms.detail: Talk to Garkor and watch the cutscene, then talk to him again to receive the 10th squad sigil.

atoms.label: Prepare for the Jungle Demon
atoms.detail: Prepare combat equipment for the final fight — good food, prayer potions, and high-healing food are strongly recommended.

atoms.label: Defeat the Jungle Demon
atoms.detail: Equip the 10th squad sigil, then fight the Jungle Demon. Use Protect from Magic and stay out of melee range to minimise damage.

atoms.label: Report victory to King Narnode
atoms.detail: Return to the Grand Tree and talk to King Narnode Shareen to complete the quest.

checkpoints.label: Well I'll be a monkey's uncle

steps.detail: Pick a method: Offer dragon bones at a gilded altar / Offer dragon bones at the Chaos Temple altar / Reanimate ensouled heads near the Dark Altar.

methods.method: Offer dragon bones at a gilded altar
methods.location: player-owned house gilded altar (or a hosted altar, e.g. worlds 330/465/512 at Rimmington)
methods.notes: Page states 643,000 xp/hr for dragon bones with both incense burners lit; a hosted altar removes the Construction 75 requirement.

methods.method: Offer dragon bones at the Chaos Temple altar
methods.location: Chaos Temple, level 38 Wilderness (multi-combat, player-killer risk)
methods.notes: Page states up to 860,000 xp/hr bringing noted dragon bones (500,000 xp/hr if suiciding each trip instead); cheaper than the gilded altar but carries PK risk.

methods.method: Reanimate ensouled heads near the Dark Altar
methods.location: Arceuus, Dark Altar (fairy ring code CIS)
methods.notes: Slower, safer alternative fed by ensouled heads gathered as monster drops rather than bought; page only states a concrete hourly rate for the high-Magic ensouled dragon head option.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw dragon bones
atoms.detail: Withdraw banked dragon bones (gathered non-GE, see next atom).

atoms.label: Kill green dragons for dragon bones (non-GE)
atoms.detail: Green dragons (Wilderness, combat 79) drop Dragon bones 100% of the time, per the monster's own drop table -- shares the exact same kill activity as train-crafting-54's dragonhide gather earlier in this segment (both drops fire from one kill).

hints.note: Wilderness PK risk -- hop if contested or under threat.

atoms.label: Offer dragon bones at a player-owned house gilded altar
atoms.detail: Best xp/hr (643,000) of the row's own 3 methods, but gated behind Construction 75 for the altar itself.

atoms.label: Offer dragon bones at the Chaos Temple altar (Wilderness)
atoms.detail: Level 38 Wilderness, multi-combat, PK risk -- no Construction requirement. Highest raw xp/hr (860,000) of the 3 methods.

hints.note: Wilderness multi-combat PK risk -- hop if contested.

atoms.label: Reanimate ensouled heads near the Dark Altar (Arceuus)
atoms.detail: No Construction requirement -- the safe fallback for an account without a gilded altar yet. Fairy ring code CIS per the row's own method.

checkpoints.label: Dragon-bone gather + altar offering, routing branch (to 52)

steps.detail: Pick a method: Ancient Magicks bursting (Smoke Burst, then Ice Burst) / High Level Alchemy / Exploiting elemental weakness on crabs (Frost Crabs, 100% Fire weakness) / Tele-alching (High Level Alchemy chained with a teleport).

hints.note: Smoke Burst usable from 62; Ice Burst better from 70 — exact xp/h not sourced.

methods.method: Ancient Magicks bursting (Smoke Burst, then Ice Burst)
methods.location: Multi-combat spawn (per-monster; page does not list a crab-specific burst spot)
methods.notes: Smoke Burst usable from 62, Ice Burst stronger from 70 once Desert Treasure I unlocks Ancient Magicks; the page's own burst-location list (Slayer/Maniacal monkeys/Zombie pirates/Bandits) does not include crabs despite this row's 'at Crabs' label.

methods.location: Anywhere, on noted items
methods.notes: The 'Alching' half of this row's label; up to ~78,000 xp/h stationary. Per project convention, source items to alch via drops/production, not Grand Exchange purchase.

methods.method: Exploiting elemental weakness on crabs (Frost Crabs, 100% Fire weakness)
methods.location: Ruins of Tapoyauik, Varlamore
methods.notes: Closest documented actual crab-killing option matching this row's 'at Crabs' phrasing; Fire spells hit roughly double damage on the 100% weakness.

methods.method: Tele-alching (High Level Alchemy chained with a teleport)
methods.location: Camelot or Ardougne teleport spot
methods.notes: Cast High Alchemy then a teleport within 3 ticks so both grant xp; Camelot is the cheapest routing at ~144,600 xp/h, Ardougne is faster but pricier per the page.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw High Level Alchemy loadout
atoms.detail: Same loadout as train-magic-55's tmg55-01 (nature rune 1/cast + fire rune 5/cast) plus own-produced fodder from earlier smithing/farming lanes -- continuing the exact non-GE alch loop past its 55 threshold rather than minting a second one (U8).

atoms.label: Cast High Level Alchemy on own-produced items until Magic 70
atoms.detail: Continuation of tmg55-02's loop (own-produced fodder, never GE-bought) with the until-threshold raised to 70. 65xp/cast, up to 78,000xp/h stationary per Pay-to-play_Magic_training.s14.

hints.note: Same non-GE fodder loop as tmg55-02 -- alch the chain's own smithed/farmed output, never a bought batch.

atoms.label: Alt: Ancient Magicks bursting (dust devils, Catacombs of Kourend)
atoms.detail: Smoke Burst usable from Magic 62 (Ancient Magicks, requires Desert Treasure I); dust devils in the Catacombs of Kourend named as a documented cheap alternative to maniacal monkeys for players without Monkey Madness II, needing 65 Slayer. Corrects the coarse row's own atom.target='crab' -- 'crab' is not documented anywhere in Pay-to-play_Magic_training.s17's own bursting-spot list (maniacal monkeys, zombie pirates, bandits, dust devils, Temple Spiders, TzHaar-Ket) nor is the page's only crab-adjacent method (Frost Crabs, s8) valid for this band: s8's own section header states 'Levels 1-55', not 55-70 as the coarse row's methods[] mislabels it.

hints.note: No dust-devil-specific xp/hr figure stated on Pay-to-play_Magic_training.s17; left ?? rather than guessed.

checkpoints.label: High Level Alchemy on own-produced fodder (continued to 70), Ancient Magicks bursting optional

steps.detail: Pick a method: Harpoon fishing swordfish/tuna (banked, no tick manipulation) / AFK Barbarian Fishing / 2-tick harpoon swordfish/tuna.

methods.method: Harpoon fishing swordfish/tuna (banked, no tick manipulation)
methods.location: Catherby

items: harpoon (or bare hands after Barbarian Training miniquest)

methods.notes: Matches the row's own label. Caveat: the current training page's only harpoon-swordfish/tuna coverage is the advanced 2-tick technique (Port Piscarilius/Fishing Guild PvP world), not a plain banked Catherby variant, so no page-stated xp/hr applies to this simpler version -- left ??.

methods.location: Otto's Grotto pond

items: feathers or fish offcuts/roe/caviar as bait

methods.notes: Page states 30 Agility/Strength needed for leaping salmon and requires partial completion of the Barbarian Training miniquest (not a full quest, so left out of reqs.quests). AFK total xp/hr per page table: 43,800 at level 58 rising to 56,800 at level 70 -- left ?? rather than averaged.

methods.location: Port Piscarilius (or Fishing Guild PvP world)

items: harpoon (crystal harpoon recommended for catch rate)

methods.notes: Page says this becomes the fastest method overall but only outpaces other options from level 61 onward. Stated xp/hr at level 61: 69,600 (normal harpoon)/79,900 (dragon harpoon); no crystal-harpoon figure until level 71 (101,800). Left ?? rather than picking one figure for the whole 58-70 band.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw harpoon
atoms.detail: Withdraw a harpoon (bare hands works after the Barbarian Training miniquest, per the route's own method note).

atoms.label: Go to the Catherby swordfish/tuna spot
atoms.detail: Catherby, tile (2834,3445) per the route's own map pin.

atoms.label: Harpoon swordfish/tuna until Fishing 70
atoms.detail: Banked harpoon fishing, no tick manipulation required.

atoms.label: Alt: AFK Barbarian Fishing (Otto's Grotto)
atoms.detail: Requires Agility 30 + Strength 30, a barbarian rod, and feathers or fish offcuts/roe/caviar as bait.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Moss giant / Nightmare Zone (Normal Rumble) / Wilderness Slayer / Ogre.

hints.note: No single wiki-cited monster fetched for this exact band; Nightmare Zone (needs 5 quests, requirement list unverified) is the wiki's documented 50-99 AFK/fastest method.

methods.location: Catacombs of Kourend, Varrock Sewers, or west of the Fishing Guild
methods.notes: Fair Hitpoints for its level with a chance at a Mossy key (Bryophyta access); hits hard on low-Defence/low-armour players.

methods.location: North of Yanille, north-west of the bank

quests: Any 5 of the ~30 quests Nightmare Zone accepts (see Nightmare_Zone.s3 for the full list)

methods.notes: Instanced and safe (deaths only drop arena loot); absorption/overload potions from points reduce food/potion costs; entry needs a coin fee.

methods.location: Wilderness
methods.notes: Slayer helm accuracy/damage bonuses make Wilderness Slayer assignments good melee xp while also banking Slayer points quickly; player-vs-player danger applies.

methods.location: East of Castle Wars, west of Yanille
methods.notes: High-Hitpoints, low-Defence target with automatic aggro; a popular cannon spot so can be crowded.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw Moss Giant loadout
atoms.detail: Withdraw food ??x and best-available weapon/armour for the attack style being trained.

atoms.label: Go to Moss giants (west of the Fishing Guild)
atoms.detail: Surface spot, members-only, no key/quest gate (unlike the Catacombs of Kourend or Brimhaven Dungeon alternatives on the same page).

atoms.label: Kill Moss giants until Attack 70
atoms.detail: Always drops big bones (bury for Prayer XP or bank for the prayer-pot-supply chain). Level 42, max hit 6, Crush attack, 50% Fire weakness.

hints.note: Big bones always drop -- bank for train-prayer-74's dragon-bones-adjacent Prayer training or bury on the spot.
Popular F2P/early-P2P training spot; hop worlds if contested.
Trains attack/strength/defence simultaneously depending on combat style used -- the coarse route models these as three sequential bands, but a real player likely trains all three concurrently here.

atoms.label: Bank: deposit Moss Giant loot
atoms.detail: Deposit big bones, coins, and any weapon/armour drops.

atoms.label: Alt: Wilderness Slayer tasks
atoms.detail: Wilderness Slayer trains combat and Slayer simultaneously (see train-slayer-50 later in this same segment) but carries PK risk in the Wilderness.

hints.note: No xp/hr figure stated on Pay-to-play_Melee_training.s14 for this method.

checkpoints.label: Moss giant routing (west of Fishing Guild)

steps.detail: Pick a method: Moss giant / Wilderness Slayer / Nightmare Zone (Normal Rumble).

methods.location: Catacombs of Kourend, Varrock Sewers, or west of the Fishing Guild
methods.notes: Fair Hitpoints for its level with a chance at a Mossy key (Bryophyta access); hits hard on low-Defence/low-armour players.

methods.location: Wilderness
methods.notes: Slayer helm accuracy/damage bonuses make Wilderness Slayer assignments good melee xp while also banking Slayer points quickly; player-vs-player danger applies.

methods.location: North of Yanille, north-west of the bank

quests: Any 5 of the ~30 quests Nightmare Zone accepts (see Nightmare_Zone.s3 for the full list)

methods.notes: Instanced and safe (deaths only drop arena loot); absorption/overload potions from points reduce food/potion costs; entry needs a coin fee.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw Moss Giant loadout
atoms.detail: Withdraw food ??x and best-available weapon/armour for the attack style being trained.

atoms.label: Go to Moss giants (west of the Fishing Guild)
atoms.detail: Surface spot, members-only, no key/quest gate (unlike the Catacombs of Kourend or Brimhaven Dungeon alternatives on the same page).

atoms.label: Kill Moss giants until Strength 70
atoms.detail: Always drops big bones (bury for Prayer XP or bank for the prayer-pot-supply chain). Level 42, max hit 6, Crush attack, 50% Fire weakness.

hints.note: Big bones always drop -- bank for train-prayer-74's dragon-bones-adjacent Prayer training or bury on the spot.
Popular F2P/early-P2P training spot; hop worlds if contested.
Trains attack/strength/defence simultaneously depending on combat style used -- the coarse route models these as three sequential bands, but a real player likely trains all three concurrently here.

atoms.label: Bank: deposit Moss Giant loot
atoms.detail: Deposit big bones, coins, and any weapon/armour drops.

atoms.label: Alt: Wilderness Slayer tasks
atoms.detail: Wilderness Slayer trains combat and Slayer simultaneously (see train-slayer-50 later in this same segment) but carries PK risk in the Wilderness.

hints.note: No xp/hr figure stated on Pay-to-play_Melee_training.s14 for this method.

checkpoints.label: Moss giant routing (west of Fishing Guild)

steps.detail: Pick a method: Wilderness Slayer / Nightmare Zone (Normal Rumble) / Moss giant.

methods.location: Wilderness
methods.notes: Slayer helm accuracy/damage bonuses make Wilderness Slayer assignments good melee xp while also banking Slayer points quickly; player-vs-player danger applies.

methods.location: North of Yanille, north-west of the bank

quests: Any 5 of the ~30 quests Nightmare Zone accepts (see Nightmare_Zone.s3 for the full list)

methods.notes: Instanced and safe (deaths only drop arena loot); absorption/overload potions from points reduce food/potion costs; entry needs a coin fee.

methods.location: Catacombs of Kourend, Varrock Sewers, or west of the Fishing Guild
methods.notes: Fair Hitpoints for its level with a chance at a Mossy key (Bryophyta access); hits hard on low-Defence/low-armour players.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw Moss Giant loadout
atoms.detail: Withdraw food ??x and best-available weapon/armour for the attack style being trained.

atoms.label: Go to Moss giants (west of the Fishing Guild)
atoms.detail: Surface spot, members-only, no key/quest gate (unlike the Catacombs of Kourend or Brimhaven Dungeon alternatives on the same page).

atoms.label: Kill Moss giants until Defence 70
atoms.detail: Always drops big bones (bury for Prayer XP or bank for the prayer-pot-supply chain). Level 42, max hit 6, Crush attack, 50% Fire weakness.

hints.note: Big bones always drop -- bank for train-prayer-74's dragon-bones-adjacent Prayer training or bury on the spot.
Popular F2P/early-P2P training spot; hop worlds if contested.
Trains attack/strength/defence simultaneously depending on combat style used -- the coarse route models these as three sequential bands, but a real player likely trains all three concurrently here.

atoms.label: Bank: deposit Moss Giant loot
atoms.detail: Deposit big bones, coins, and any weapon/armour drops.

atoms.label: Alt: Wilderness Slayer tasks
atoms.detail: Wilderness Slayer trains combat and Slayer simultaneously (see train-slayer-50 later in this same segment) but carries PK risk in the Wilderness.

hints.note: No xp/hr figure stated on Pay-to-play_Melee_training.s14 for this method.

checkpoints.label: Moss giant routing (west of Fishing Guild)

steps.detail: Obtain orange and blue goblin mail armour for the goblin generals. Craft dyes from berries/onions via Aggie in Draynor.

hints.note: inspect the frozen goblin generals in the Lumbridge Castle dining room to start
Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

refs.title: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin generals
Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin generals

media.caption: Lumbridge Castle dining room: General Wartface's plinth, General Bentnoze alongside (rfd-goblins / goblin generals)

atoms.label: Inspect the frozen goblin generals to start
atoms.detail: Inspect the frozen General Wartface/Bentnoze statues in the Lumbridge Castle dining room to begin this chapter.

refs.title: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin generals

atoms.label: Head into Goblin Village and down to the cellar
atoms.detail: Enter the building just south of the two generals in Goblin Village and climb down the ladder to find Mudknuckles, the goblin cook.

refs.title: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin generals

atoms.label: Talk to Mudknuckles
atoms.detail: Mudknuckles explains he needs charcoal to finish a cooking recipe he overheard two dwarves discussing.

hints.note: conversation about the overheard dwarf recipe; exact option text not stated on this chapter's own page

refs.title: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin generals

atoms.label: Buy charcoal
atoms.detail: Buy charcoal or ground charcoal from Shilo Village, Jiminua's Jungle Store, or Gunslik's Assorted Items (the three named vendors on this chapter's item list).

refs.title: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin generals

atoms.label: Give the charcoal to Mudknuckles
atoms.detail: Hand the charcoal to Mudknuckles; he adds it to his cauldron, which explodes in a slapstick cutscene that you both survive.

refs.title: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin generals

atoms.label: Gather dye east of the Khazard spirit tree
atoms.detail: Pick up a ground dye spawn (any colour except red, yellow, orange or pink -- purple works) directly east of the spirit tree at the Khazard Battlefield, south of Ardougne Castle.

refs.title: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin generals

atoms.label: Dye an orange to make a dyed orange
atoms.detail: Slice an orange with a knife, then use the dye on the slices to make a dyed orange. The chapter's own item list names the orange as a required item but does not state where to source it, so its vendor is left unresolved.

refs.title: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin generals

atoms.label: Buy spice at the Ardougne market
atoms.detail: Buy (or gather via thieving) spice or gnome spice from the Ardougne market, from the Culinaromancer's Chest, or in the Tree Gnome Stronghold.

refs.title: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin generals

atoms.label: Make spicy maggots
atoms.detail: Use the spice on fishing bait to make spicy maggots. The chapter's own item list names fishing bait as required but does not state a vendor for it here.

refs.title: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin generals

atoms.label: Make soggy bread
atoms.detail: Use a bucket of water on bread to make soggy bread. Neither the bread nor the bucket of water has a stated vendor on this chapter's own page.

refs.title: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin generals

atoms.label: Deliver the three doctored ingredients
atoms.detail: Give the dyed orange, spicy maggots and soggy bread to Mudknuckles; he hands back the Slop of compromise.

refs.title: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin generals

atoms.label: Return to the Lumbridge Castle dining room
atoms.detail: Bring the Slop of compromise back to the goblin generals in Lumbridge.

refs.title: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin generals

atoms.label: Feed the Slop of compromise to the generals
atoms.detail: Give the Slop of compromise to the goblin generals to free them and complete the chapter.

refs.title: Recipe for Disaster/Freeing the Goblin generals

checkpoints.label: Inspect the goblin generals to start
Find Mudknuckles and trigger the cauldron explosion
Deliver the food and free the generals

steps.detail: Loot coins from nearby goblins/men opportunistically for the scimitar fund (amount ??). Cowhides optional: bank a stack for early Crafting if passing Al Kharid bank anyway.

hints.note: Cowhide branch is optional — skip if inventory churn slows kills.

media.caption: Arrive at the Lumbridge East cow field (ctr-02-kill-cows)
Cows in the pen around the player; these are the kill target

steps.detail: Cook a snake on a plinth for Awowogei on Ape Atoll. Requires Monkey Madness I completion.

mapMarkers.label: Awowogei's table, Lumbridge Castle dining room (start, {{Quest details|startmap=}}-sourced)

hints.note: inspect Awowogei in the Lumbridge Castle dining room to start
Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

media.caption: Lumbridge Castle dining room: Awowogei's plinth (rfd-awowogei / Snake on a Plinth)

atoms.label: Inspect Awowogei in the Lumbridge Castle dining room
atoms.detail: Start the subquest by inspecting Awowogei's table.

mapMarkers.label: Awowogei's table, Lumbridge Castle dining room (quest start)

atoms.label: Travel to Ape Atoll
atoms.detail: Use fairy ring code CLR (lands mid agility-course) or speak to Daero on the Grand Tree's first floor.

atoms.label: Enter the city with a greegree equipped
atoms.detail: Head north with any greegree equipped (a Karamjan monkey greegree works if no others are held) and pass through the gate; skip the rest of this greegree-crafting checkpoint entirely if the ninja, zombie and gorilla greegrees were already obtained during Monkey Madness I.

hints.value: attempting the agility rope swing without the ninja greegree drops you into jail; pick the lock to leave

atoms.label: Buy monkey talismans from Tutab
atoms.detail: Buy three monkey talismans from the rune salesman Tutab for 1,000 coins each, or get them free from the Monkey Child.

atoms.label: Kill a Monkey Guard for its bones
atoms.detail: Fight a Monkey Guard near the prayer altar with ranged or magic from a safespot; melee works but the guard self-heals at low health.

hints.value: north-east corner behind the fire, or south behind the ladder

atoms.label: Kill a Monkey Archer for its bones
atoms.detail: Fight one of the two ninja monkeys (monkey archers) north of the bank icon, protecting from range or safespotting in the tall grass.

hints.value: stand in the tall grass north of the bank icon

atoms.label: Kill a zombie monkey for its bones
atoms.detail: Fight a zombie monkey inside the Ape Atoll Dungeon near Zooknock, watching for traps and poisonous spiders.

atoms.label: Trade bones and talismans to Zooknock for the three greegrees
atoms.detail: Give Zooknock a monkey talisman plus one set of bones at a time, repeating for all three bone types, to craft the ninja monkey, zombie monkey and gorilla greegrees.

hints.value: repeat the bones+talisman exchange with Zooknock three times, once per bone type

atoms.label: Cross the Ape Atoll Agility Course to the palace
atoms.detail: Wear the ninja monkey greegree and complete the course over the bridge south of town; 48 Agility is required and cannot be boosted.

atoms.label: Enter Awowogei's palace
atoms.detail: Talk to the Elder Guard to be let in; the quest must already be started by inspecting Awowogei for this to work.

atoms.label: Ask Awowogei about his favourite food
atoms.detail: He refuses to say directly but mentions three wise monkeys in the temple who might know.

atoms.label: Travel to the temple north of Awowogei
atoms.detail: Head to the temple's north-west ground-floor corner to find the three wise monkeys.

atoms.label: Buy monkey nuts and a banana
atoms.detail: Buy monkey nuts (about 13 coins) if not already carried, and grab a normal banana.

atoms.label: Tell the three wise monkeys about the surprise party
atoms.detail: Explain the plan, then speak to them again with the banana and again with the monkey nuts; they reject both as unworthy and demand a sacred red banana and tchiki monkey nuts instead.

hints.value: all three monkeys must be spoken to or the red banana tree never spawns

atoms.label: Travel to the red banana tree
atoms.detail: Wear the gorilla greegree for the strength needed, then head to the tree on the north-west coast, south-west of the gnome glider.

atoms.label: Pull red bananas from the tree with a rope
atoms.detail: Use a rope on the tree at least twice, since a wise monkey eats one of the sliced bananas brought back to them; buy rope from Ifaba's general store if none is carried.

hints.value: if the tree is bare, revisit the three monkeys with a plain banana to trigger a respawn

atoms.label: Slice the red bananas
atoms.detail: Slice the red bananas with a knife into sliced red bananas.

hints.value: keep an extra sliced red banana as a backup against burning the snake

atoms.label: Continue the agility course to the tchiki nut bush
atoms.detail: Wear the ninja monkey greegree and continue the course past the rope swing to the broken hut around a hole in the ground.

atoms.label: Pick tchiki monkey nuts from the bush
atoms.detail: Climb down into the hole and pick tchiki monkey nuts from the bush.

hints.value: if the bush is bare, revisit the three monkeys with plain monkey nuts to trigger a respawn

atoms.label: Grind the tchiki nuts into paste
atoms.detail: Grind the tchiki monkey nuts into paste with a pestle and mortar.

hints.value: grind extra paste as a backup against burning the snake

atoms.label: Travel to the Crash Island snake pit
atoms.detail: Leave Ape Atoll for Crash Island, head east to the large hole in the ground, and enter the cave below.

atoms.label: Kill a level 84 Big Snake for its corpse
atoms.detail: Turn on Protect from Melee, then kill a Big Snake and take its corpse; kill a few extra as backup in case the cooking step burns one.

hints.value: enable Protect from Melee before entering the pit
collect several snake corpses as insurance against burning

atoms.label: Stuff the snake corpse
atoms.detail: Stuff the snake corpse with a sliced red banana and tchiki nut paste to make a raw stuffed snake.

atoms.label: Reach the hot coals inside the temple
atoms.detail: Go down the trapdoor past the gorilla guards, down a ramp, and through the crack in the wall between the two bamboo ramps to the hot coals patch.

atoms.label: Cook the stuffed snake past the hot coals
atoms.detail: Wear the zombie monkey greegree so the hot coals do not damage you, then cook the stuffed snake on the long rock, boosting Cooking if needed.

hints.value: overcooking the snake is possible near the Cooking level requirement

atoms.label: Return to the Lumbridge Castle dining room
atoms.detail: Travel back with the m'speak amulet equipped so Awowogei can understand you.

atoms.label: Give the cooked stuffed snake to Awowogei
atoms.detail: Use the stuffed snake on Awowogei without eating it to complete the subquest.

checkpoints.label: Travel to Ape Atoll and enter
Craft the three greegrees (skip if already held from Monkey Madness I)
Learn Awowogei's favourite food from the three wise monkeys
Gather the red banana and tchiki monkey nuts
Hunt a big snake and stuff it
Cook the snake past the hot coals and deliver it

steps.detail: Semi-consistent GP and gear. Entry-level bossing in Morytania.

mapMarkers.label: Barrows entrance (six burial mounds, east of Mort'ton)

hints.note: List order = fastest-to-slowest per the wiki's own ranking. Minigame Teleport to Shades of Mort'ton needs the Shades of Mort'ton quest but is free/no inventory slot, 20-min cooldown.

steps.detail: Pickpocket the Zealot's key, descend through the Abandoned Mine's cave levels, solve a lever/points puzzle to route a minecart carrying a light-giving glowing fungus, retrieve the fungus, open a water valve to access a lift, then fight Treus Dayth (level 95 but notably harder than the level suggests -- ranged/magic + Protect from Missiles recommended, not a safe death fight since the fungus is lost on death) before cutting a crystal outcrop to finish.

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Speak to the Zealot outside the mine.
atoms.detail: He stands guard at the mine entrance.

hints.value: 1I follow the path of Saradomin.|1I come seeking challenges and quests.|1What quest is that then?|3Why was everyone in the mines slaughtered?|1Is there any other way into the mine?|1Where is the second entrance to the mines?|1Can I borrow your key?

atoms.label: Pickpocket the Zealot for his key.
atoms.detail: Thieving level not stated on the fetched page.

atoms.label: Climb over the cart south of the Zealot and crawl down the cart tunnel.
atoms.detail: This is the way into the Abandoned Mine.

atoms.label: Cross cave level 1 and descend to level 2.
atoms.detail: Run west through the cave, crawl through the cart tunnel, re-enter via the southern entrance, and descend the nearby ladder.

atoms.label: Go down the ladder directly east.
atoms.detail: This leads from cave level 2 to level 3.

atoms.label: Cross cave level 3 to the ladder down to level 4.
atoms.detail: Avoid touching the moving mine carts; go south along the tracks using the alcove for cover, then slightly west, then climb down the ladder.

atoms.label: Pick a glowing fungus.
atoms.detail: Avoid any random event that would teleport you out from here on, or the fungus turns to ash.

atoms.label: Place the fungus in the searchable cart.
atoms.detail: The cart carries the fungus down to light the way.

atoms.label: Check the points-settings board for the levers that need changing.
atoms.detail: The starting lever layout (A-K) is randomized per player; the board shows the target vs current configuration.

atoms.label: Pull the required levers in the south-east bank (A-E).
atoms.detail: Set per the board reading from the previous atom.

atoms.label: Pull the required levers in the north-west bank (F-K).
atoms.detail: Set per the board reading.

atoms.label: Return to the points board and start the cart.
atoms.detail: If the cart doesn't reach the ladder, grab another fungus and redo the puzzle.

atoms.label: Backtrack out of cave level 4 the way you came.
atoms.detail: Up the ladder by the fungus spawn, north past the haunted cart, east and up a ladder, west and up another ladder, then west to exit the cave.

atoms.label: Follow the tunnels and ladders back to the cart.
atoms.detail: Crawl down the north cart tunnel, climb down the nearby ladder, go north-east and climb down the ladder by the door, then west then north and climb down the final ladder.

atoms.label: Search the cart for the fungus and take it.
atoms.detail: Do not drop the fungus until the quest is finished.

atoms.label: Climb up the ladder, then head east and south past the moving cart.
atoms.detail: Start conserving run energy here for the timed dash to the lift.

atoms.label: Use the Zealot's key on the water valve to open it.
atoms.detail: Grab the chisel near the lift first if you don't already have one -- there is no time to fetch it after opening the valve.

atoms.label: Quickly run to the lift and take it down.
atoms.detail: The valve start a timer before it floods.

atoms.label: Go south, then east and down the stairs, then through the door.
atoms.detail: This leads to Treus Dayth.

atoms.label: Pick up the innocent-looking key to start the boss fight.
atoms.detail: This triggers Treus Dayth's appearance.

atoms.label: Defeat Treus Dayth.
atoms.detail: Level 95; pray Protect from Missiles throughout. Force him into the north-east corner, then retreat west along the detached shaft to fight from range, avoiding his teleports and crane damage; Crumble Undead is noted as especially effective. Not a safe fight -- dying or teleporting away loses the glowing fungus and requires fetching a fresh one.

atoms.label: Pick up the key dropped after the fight.
atoms.detail: Exact key name not stated on the fetched page.

atoms.label: Head back up the eastern stairs, then west and down the other stairs.
atoms.detail: The glowing fungus lights the way through this dark section.

atoms.label: Open the door and cut the crystal outcrop with your chisel to finish.
atoms.detail: This completes the quest.

steps.detail: Pick a method: Offer wyvern/dragon bones at a gilded altar / Offer dragon bones at the Chaos Temple altar / Reanimate ensouled dragon heads near the Dark Altar.

methods.method: Offer wyvern/dragon bones at a gilded altar
methods.location: player-owned house gilded altar (or a hosted altar, e.g. worlds 330/465/512 at Rimmington)
methods.notes: Page states 643,000 xp/hr for dragon bones with both burners lit; wyvern bones share the same 72-xp-per-bone bury value on the page's own offering table but no page-stated altar hourly rate.

methods.method: Offer dragon bones at the Chaos Temple altar
methods.location: Chaos Temple, level 38 Wilderness (multi-combat, player-killer risk)
methods.notes: Page states up to 860,000 xp/hr bringing noted dragon bones (500,000 xp/hr suiciding instead); cheaper than the gilded altar but carries PK risk.

methods.method: Reanimate ensouled dragon heads near the Dark Altar
methods.location: Arceuus, Dark Altar (fairy ring code CIS)
methods.notes: Page states ~343,000 xp/hr reanimating and killing ensouled dragon heads (220/hr); heads are gathered as monster drops rather than bought, and this is the only head-tier the page gives a concrete hourly rate for.

atoms.label: Bank: withdraw dragon bones + food
atoms.detail: Dragon bones (own-farmed from earlier dragon/wyvern kills, source ??, never bought) + food ??x for the Wilderness trip.

atoms.label: Go to the Chaos Temple altar (level 38 Wilderness)
atoms.detail: Multi-combat area, player-killer risk. Coordinates not pinned by the fetched section -- left ??.

hints.note: PK risk in the Wilderness; the gilded altar alt (player-owned house, Construction 75, 643,000 xp/h) avoids this entirely but is gated on a Construction level not assumed reached here.

atoms.label: Offer dragon bones at the Chaos Temple altar until Prayer 74
atoms.detail: 860,000 xp/h per Pay-to-play_Prayer_training.s14 -- the highest-rate altar option with no skill/quest gate, at the cost of Wilderness PK exposure.

steps.detail: Synthetic step — no matching step found in bank.

methods.location: North of Yanille, north-west of the bank

quests: Any 5 of the ~30 quests Nightmare Zone accepts (see Nightmare_Zone.s3 for the full list)

methods.notes: Page calls this the fastest solo Attack/Strength xp/hr in the game once geared with Dharok's (92+ Hitpoints) or Obsidian+Berserker necklace+Toktz-Xil-Ak.

methods.notes: Slayer helm bonuses make task kills solid melee xp while banking Slayer points, though efficient point-blocking Slayer itself needs many quests.

methods.location: Slayer Tower
methods.notes: Below-average offence for its level so high-Defence players sustain easily, but above-average Defence caps the xp/hr; drops are consistently valuable rather than rare.

steps.detail: Synthetic step — no matching step found in bank.

methods.method: Bursting/Barraging on-task Slayer monsters (dust devils, Greater Nechryael, Warped Jellies, smoke devils)
methods.location: Various Slayer task assignment locations
methods.notes: Page suggests passive training to 82 then a saturated heart boost to 94 for Ice Barrage, or starting Slayer bursting at 70 with weaker spells.

methods.location: Kruk's Dungeon (Monkey Madness II, up to the Kruk's Dungeon point)
methods.notes: Page calls this the fastest viable real-time method: Ice Burst ~250,000-310,000 xp/h, Ice Barrage ~320,000-400,000 xp/h depending on active vs AFK play.

methods.location: Level 10-11 Wilderness, near the Chaos altar
methods.notes: PvP-risk wilderness option; page recommends the Accursed sceptre (a) for bonus NPC damage and the Medium Wilderness Diary for better drop value.

methods.method: Bursting/Barraging bandits at the Bandit Camp
methods.location: The Big Heist Lodge, two tiles west of the door
methods.notes: Requires a Saradomin/Zamorak item plus an Ancient Magicks autocast weapon; page states ~150,000-235,000 xp/h bursting, ~230,000-305,000 xp/h barraging.

methods.location: Varies by assignment
methods.notes: Slayer helm bonuses make task kills solid melee xp while banking Slayer points, though efficient point-blocking Slayer itself needs many quests.

methods.notes: Slayer helm bonuses make task kills solid melee xp while banking Slayer points, though efficient point-blocking Slayer itself needs many quests.

steps.detail: Grants Cooking gauntlets (stop burning food) and Goldsmith gauntlets (Smithing XP boost).

media.caption: Varrock, south of the east bank: Dimintheis (quest-family-crest start)
Player beside Dimintheis; he sends you after his three sons' crest pieces

atoms.label: Talk to Dimintheis (start Family Crest)
atoms.detail: Fenced house above the clothes shop, south-eastern Varrock. Agree to help; his three sons split the family crest into pieces decades ago and scattered; his son Caleb now cooks in Catherby.

atoms.label: Travel to Catherby
Ask Caleb for his crest piece
atoms.detail: Inside or near the sink building south of the Farming shop. Confirm his identity, ask what happened to the crest (mandatory to unlock the other pieces), then agree to bring fish-salad ingredients.

atoms.label: Gather 5 cooked fish for Caleb
atoms.detail: One each of cooked shrimp, salmon, tuna, bass, and swordfish. Ironman note (named non-GE vendors, no substitution needed for a normal account): raw fish from Frankie's Fishing Emporium (Port Piscarilius), cooked salmon from the West Ardougne General Store, cooked swordfish from the Shrimp and Parrot in Brimhaven (also a Tribal Totem reward), cooked bass from the Warrior Guild Food Shop.

atoms.label: Deliver the fish to Caleb for his crest piece
atoms.detail: He hands over his crest piece and mentions brother Avan was last seen searching the desert for treasure.

atoms.label: Ask the Al Kharid gem trader about Avan
atoms.detail: North Al Kharid, by the gnome glider. He recalls Avan searching for 'perfect gold' in the sand dunes.

atoms.label: Find Avan at the Al Kharid mine
atoms.detail: Wandering the northern Al Kharid mine pit entrance, wearing a yellow cape. He wants a ring and necklace of 'perfect gold' to win a lady's affection and mentions a dwarf named Boot who may know a source.

atoms.label: Ask Boot about perfect gold
atoms.detail: Down the trapdoor behind the eastern building of the Dwarven Mine (west of Edgeville Monastery); Boot wanders south-west of the entrance by the rock columns. He points to the Witchaven Dungeon east of Ardougne. Must be spoken to before Avan will accept the finished jewellery, even though it can technically be mined/smithed beforehand.

atoms.label: Solve the Witchaven Dungeon lever puzzle
atoms.detail: Enter and head north-east past hobgoblin/ogre rooms (Protect from Melee negates all their damage; no kills required). Six-step sequence to permanently unlock the hellhound room: north-room outer lever up; south room via east door, inner lever up, exit west door; north room outer lever down then inner lever up; north room outer lever up again; south room via west door, lever down, exit east door.

atoms.label: Mine 2 perfect gold ore
atoms.detail: In the unlocked hellhound room, mine the gold rocks (Mining 40) while zigzagging the level-122 hellhounds' short aggro range; a safespot exists behind the rock column south of the entrance if aggro is drawn (world-hop to reset a mined rock).

atoms.label: Smelt and smith the perfect ring and necklace
atoms.detail: At any furnace (Al Kharid is convenient for the Avan hand-off): smelt the 2 perfect gold ore into bars (Smithing 40), then use the ring mould and necklace mould with 2 cut rubies to smith a perfect ring and perfect necklace (Crafting 40) -- do not pick the plain gold jewellery menu options.

atoms.label: Give Avan the jewellery for his crest piece
atoms.detail: He hands over his crest piece and directs you to Johnathon at the Jolly Boar Inn north-east of Varrock.

atoms.label: Cure Johnathon with an antipoison potion
atoms.detail: 1F of the Jolly Boar Inn, north-west room. He's delirious from a poisonous spider bite -- use any antipoison potion on him (fully consumed regardless of dose).

atoms.label: Weaken and kill Chronozon for the last crest piece
atoms.detail: Johnathon lost the crest fighting Chronozon (level 170) in the Wilderness section of Edgeville Dungeon, by the poison spiders near the Earth obelisk. Cast Wind, Water, Earth, and Fire Blast, landing one confirmed hit each ('Chronozon weakens...' in chat), in any order, then finish with any style. A safespot exists one tile in front of the eastern standing torch.

atoms.label: Reassemble the crest and return it to Dimintheis
atoms.detail: Combine the three crest pieces, return to Varrock, and hand the crest to Dimintheis. Quest complete -- pick one of Caleb's/Avan's/Johnathon's gauntlet imbuements.

steps.detail: Unlocks Zanaris and cosmic rune altar. Grants access to dragon weaponry.

hints.note: start: ask about Zanaris

media.caption: NW Lumbridge Swamp: the Warrior (quest-lost-city start)
Player beside the Warrior; ask about Zanaris to start the quest

atoms.label: Ask the Warrior about Zanaris
atoms.detail: Speak to the Warrior camped north-west of Lumbridge Swamp and ask what he's guarding and who Zanaris is.

atoms.label: Chop (don't chop down) the nearby tree
atoms.detail: Go to the tree closest to the Warrior's encampment and right-click it, choosing the 'chop' option rather than 'chop down'. This makes Shamus appear.

atoms.label: Speak to Shamus
atoms.detail: Talk to Shamus about how the camp fits in a shed.

atoms.label: Bank weapons and armour before Entrana
atoms.detail: Deposit all weapons and armour at a bank (jewellery such as a ring of dueling, amulet of glory, or combat bracelet is allowed) — Entrana's monks confiscate anything else. Optionally buy a chronicle from Diango and charge it with teleport cards if you lack a teleport to Entrana (not required, just convenient for repeat trips).

atoms.label: Travel to the Port Sarim docks
atoms.detail: Head to the Port Sarim docks, south of the Rusty Anchor Inn.

atoms.label: Sail to Entrana
atoms.detail: Talk to a Monk of Entrana at the docks to travel to the island.

atoms.label: Reach the cave on Entrana
atoms.detail: Cross the north-east bridge, go west to find the Cave monk, then go down the ladder. Do not craft a battlestaff or bow before entering the cave, or the monks will knock you out and send you back to Port Sarim.

atoms.label: Farm zombies for a bronze axe
atoms.detail: Kill zombies in the cave until one drops a bronze axe (roughly 40% drop chance per kill, so exact kill count is "??"). Two level-92 Greater Demons occupy the room before the Dramen tree — be careful.

atoms.label: Reach the Dramen tree
atoms.detail: Go south-east past the Greater Demons and attempt to chop the Dramen tree. Caution: the nearby magical door teleports you into level-32 Wilderness.

atoms.label: Defeat the Dramen Tree Spirit
atoms.detail: Chopping the tree summons the Dramen Tree Spirit; kill it (there's a safespot behind the southern fungus formation). Do not use the mushroom near the demons as a safespot — even after killing the spirit once, it respawns the next time you try to chop.

atoms.label: Chop dramen branches
atoms.detail: Chop the now-unguarded Dramen tree with the bronze axe. Cutting at least three branches is recommended — spares are useful later for the Freeing Sir Amik Varze RFD subquest and for smuggling into Thorvald's trial in the Fremennik Trials — but don't make them into staves yet.

atoms.label: Carve a dramen staff
atoms.detail: Use a knife on a dramen branch to make a dramen staff.

atoms.label: Return to the Lumbridge Swamp tool shed
atoms.detail: Teleport out of the cave, then travel to the tool shed in the centre of Lumbridge Swamp.

atoms.label: Enter Zanaris
atoms.detail: Wield the dramen staff and enter the tool shed — it opens onto Zanaris and completes the quest.

checkpoints.label: Cave trial: bronze axe and the Dramen Tree Spirit
Craft the dramen staff and enter Zanaris

steps.instruction: Complete In Aid of the Myreque
steps.detail: Myreque chain quest 2: rebuild Burgh de Rott (repair pub basement, general store, bank, furnace) to unlock its bank, kill Gadderanks + juvinates guarding the town, escort Ivan Strom through a Temple-Trekking-style route fighting vampyre juvinates, then craft the Rod of Ivandis (silver+mithril bar, sapphire, soft clay mould, enchant) and deliver it to Veliaf.

refs.title: In Aid of the Myreque/Quick guide

hints.note: Quest reward XP — counts toward your skills, replacing that much training.

atoms.label: Talk to Aurel again to receive a crate
atoms.detail: If asked for items you dislike before receiving the crate, walk away to re-roll the request.

atoms.detail: The exact items requested vary.

atoms.detail: Needs a silver weapon, Wolfbane dagger, or Efaritay's aid to damage them.

atoms.label: Talk to Polmafi Ferdygris
atoms.detail: Bank any followers first -- Ivan Strom refuses to leave if you have one, even in your inventory.

refs.title: In Aid of the Myreque/Quick guide

atoms.label: Give Ivan Strom food and steel armour before departing (recommended, not required)
atoms.detail: He only accepts snail/salmon/slimy eel/stew for food and a steel med helm, steel chainbody, and steel platelegs for armour.

refs.title: In Aid of the Myreque/Quick guide

atoms.label: Talk to Ivan Strom to begin the escort, choosing the short or long route
atoms.detail: Route 1 features two level-75 Vampyre Juvinates; route 2 features four level-50 Juvinates. A pet cannot come along and must be banked.

refs.title: In Aid of the Myreque/Quick guide

atoms.label: Kill the Vampyre Juvinates escorting Ivan before they kill him
atoms.detail: Alternate attacks between them to keep both focused on you; talking to Ivan may also stop a vampyre attacking him.

hints.value: Juvinate count is route-dependent (2 on the short route, 4 on the long route); modelled as a gte/until threshold rather than a fixed count since the route choice is the player's.

refs.title: In Aid of the Myreque/Quick guide

hints.value: Veliaf told me about Ivandis. / Is there somewhere that I might get more information about Ivandis? / The lives of those pitiful few left in Morytania could rest on this!

atoms.detail: The book's title reads 'The Sleeping Seven' at the top.

refs.title: In Aid of the Myreque/Quick guide
